papers.title: PlanBench-XL: Evaluating Long-Horizon Planning of LLM Tool-Use Agents in Large-Scale Tool Ecosystems
papers.abstract: Abstract PlanBench-XL evaluates large language model agents' ability to plan and adapt in complex tool-rich environments with limited visibility and dynamic disruptions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM agents increasingly operate in large tool ecosystems, where real-world tasks require discovering relevant tools, inferring implicit sub-goals, and adapting to dynamic environments over long horizons. However, existing benchmarks rarely evaluate planning under retrieval-limited tool visibility. To address this gap, we introduce PlanBench-XL, an interactive benchmark of 327 retail tasks over 1,665 tools that tests whether agents can iteratively retrieve usable tools, invoke them to uncover intermediate evidence for subsequent calls toward the final goal. PlanBench-XL further features an optional blocking mechanism that simulates real-world unpredictability through missing, failing, or distracting tool functions, forcing agents to detect disrupted paths and adapt at runtime. Experiments on ten leading LLMs show that massive-tool planning remains challenging: while GPT-5.4 achieves 51.90% accuracy in block-free settings, it collapses to 11.36% under the most severe blocking condition. Further analysis shows that agents are especially vulnerable when failures lack explicit error signals or when recovery requires longer alternative tool-use paths. These results establish PlanBench-XL as a testbed for diagnosing agentic planning failures and highlight the need for robust adaptive planning in long-horizon tasks with large, imperfect tool environments.

ru.title: Обучение агентов планировать в реальных условиях с множеством несовершенных инструментов
ru.desc: PlanBench-XL — это интерактивный бенчмарк для оценки способности больших языковых моделей планировать и адаптироваться в сложных окружениях с ограниченной видимостью инструментов и динамическими сбоями. Бенчмарк содержит 327 задач в области розницы с использованием 1,665 различных инструментов, проверяя, могут ли агенты итеративно извлекать нужные инструменты и вызывать их для получения промежуточных результатов. Авторы вводят механизм блокирования, который моделирует реальную непредсказуемость через отказывающие или отвлекающие функции инструментов, заставляя агентов обнаруживать нарушения и адаптироваться во время выполнения. Эксперименты показывают, что планирование с большим набором инструментов остаётся сложной задачей: GPT-4.5 достигает 51.90% точности без помех, но падает до 11.36% при серьёзных сбоях.

en.title: Mastering Planning in Tool-Rich Environments
en.desc: PlanBench-XL is a new benchmark designed to evaluate how well large language model (LLM) agents can plan and adapt in complex environments with many tools and limited visibility. It consists of 327 retail tasks that require agents to find and use various tools while dealing with unexpected disruptions. The benchmark tests the agents' ability to retrieve tools, gather information, and adjust their strategies in real-time when faced with challenges. Results show that while some models perform well in ideal conditions, their accuracy drops significantly when they encounter tool failures or distractions, highlighting the need for better adaptive planning capabilities.

zh.title: 评估代理在复杂环境中的规划能力
zh.desc: PlanBench-XL 是一个评估大型语言模型代理在复杂工具丰富环境中规划和适应能力的基准。该基准包含327个零售任务和1665个工具，测试代理能否在有限的工具可见性下迭代检索可用工具，并在动态环境中适应。实验结果表明，尽管GPT-5.4在无阻塞条件下的准确率为51.90%，但在最严重的阻塞条件下准确率降至11.36%。这些结果强调了在大型不完美工具环境中进行长期任务时，代理需要具备强大的适应性规划能力。

papers.title: OpenRath: Session-Centered Runtime State for Agent Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract OpenRath introduces a PyTorch-like programming model for multi-agent systems using Session as a central runtime abstraction that enables explicit fork, merge, and replay operations while recording comprehensive execution state.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Modern agent systems often suffer from fragmented runtime state: transcripts, tool effects, memory events, workspace placement, branch provenance, and replay evidence are recorded separately and become difficult to inspect or reproduce. OpenRath addresses this issue with a PyTorch-like programming model for multi-agent, multi-session systems. The analogy concerns the role of a central first-class runtime abstraction, not tensor computation. Its core abstraction is Session, the runtime value passed between agents and workflows. A Session is branchable, inspectable, replayable, backend-aware, and composable. It records conversation chunks, sandbox placement, lineage metadata, token usage, pending work, and tool evidence, while defining where memory interactions enter the runtime record. Since this state is carried by the same value used in program execution, fork, merge, and replay become explicit runtime operations rather than states reconstructed from external traces. OpenRath further defines Sandbox, Tool, Agent, Memory, Workflow, and Selector, with Selector turning control flow into runtime-routed decisions. This report presents the programming model, architecture, audited milestones, and evidence protocol. Its claims are limited to controlled runtime properties, while broad quantitative comparisons, live-provider quality, optional-backend availability, and memory quality are left for follow-on evaluation. The central thesis is that Session provides agent systems with a first-class runtime value for auditable composition.

ru.title: Session как аудируемое первоклассное значение для композиции многоагентных систем
ru.desc: OpenRath представляет новую модель программирования для многоагентных систем на основе PyTorch, в которой Session служит центральной абстракцией времени выполнения. Session записывает все аспекты выполнения программы: диалоги, размещение в песочнице, метаданные происхождения и использование токенов. Система позволяет явно выполнять операции ветвления, слияния и воспроизведения программ, так как полное состояние выполнения хранится в единственном значении. OpenRath решает проблему фрагментированного состояния в современных многоагентных системах, где различные данные о выполнении записываются отдельно и становятся трудными для инспекции и воспроизведения.

en.title: Streamlining Multi-Agent Systems with Sessions
en.desc: OpenRath presents a new programming model for multi-agent systems that simplifies the management of runtime states. It introduces a central abstraction called 'Session' that allows for explicit operations like fork, merge, and replay, making it easier to track and reproduce the execution state. This model addresses the common issue of fragmented runtime information by integrating various elements such as conversation chunks and memory interactions into a single, manageable structure. By treating Session as a first-class runtime value, OpenRath enhances the audibility and composability of agent systems, paving the way for more efficient workflows.

zh.title: OpenRath：多智能体系统的全新运行时抽象
zh.desc: OpenRath提出了一种类似于PyTorch的编程模型，专注于多智能体系统，使用Session作为核心运行时抽象。这个模型解决了现代智能体系统中运行时状态碎片化的问题，使得记录的执行状态更加全面和易于检查。Session不仅可以进行分支、检查和重放操作，还能记录对话片段、工具证据和内存交互等信息。通过将这些状态与程序执行中的值结合，OpenRath使得分叉、合并和重放成为明确的运行时操作，而不是从外部痕迹重建的状态。

papers.title: DataClaw0: Agentic Tailoring Multimodal Data from Raw Streams
papers.abstract: Abstract Agentic Data Tailoring paradigm uses learnable data processing to structure high-entropy multimodal streams, with DataClaw_0-9B model achieving robust alignment through SFT and GRPO on a novel benchmark.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Massive unstructured multimodal streams suffer from high "data entropy," impeding both efficient human knowledge acquisition and high-quality AI post-training. Existing passive annotation paradigms, heavily reliant on heuristic rules or general VLMs, are costly, monotonous, and fail to unlock the deep procedural logic embedded in raw data. We elevate data processing to a learnable capability, proposing a paradigm shift towards Agentic Data Tailoring, which actively refining and structuring data to align with diverse user and downstream intents. To overcome the data scarcity bottleneck in training such high-order capabilities, we design a two-stage pipeline grounding generative semantic synthesis in deterministic Factual Anchors, yielding a large-scale dataset spanning five core physical and digital domains. Building upon this, DataClaw_0-9B model synergizes Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), achieving robust alignment with complex refinement and tailoring intents. To systematically quantify this capability, we construct DataClaw_0-val, the first benchmark dedicated to data refinement. Crucially, we adopt downstream post-training as the ultimate validation touchstone. Evaluations on video generation, real-world VQA, and GUI navigation confirm that DataClaw_0 delivers high-information-density tailored data, facilitating efficient model adaptation to new tasks under limited training data regimes. Project page: https://czjdsg.github.io/MakeAnyData
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Активная оптимизация структуры данных через обучаемых агентов
ru.desc: В работе предложена новая парадигма Agentic Data Tailoring, которая превращает обработку данных в обучаемую способность модели вместо использования статических правил. Авторы разработали модель DataClaw_0-9B, которая использует комбинацию Supervised Fine-Tuning и Group Relative Policy Optimization для активной очистки и структурирования многомодальных потоков данных высокой энтропии. Для решения проблемы дефицита данных при обучении такой сложной способности авторы создали двухэтапный pipeline на основе детерминированных факториальных якорей, что позволило собрать большой датасет. Модель показывает эффективность на задачах видеогенерации, визуального вопросно-ответного диалога и навигации по интерфейсам, обеспечивая лучшую адаптацию к новым задачам при ограниченных данных обучения.

en.title: Transforming Data Processing with Learnable Tailoring
en.desc: The paper introduces the Agentic Data Tailoring paradigm, which enhances data processing by making it learnable, allowing for better structuring of complex multimodal data streams. The DataClaw_0-9B model utilizes Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to achieve effective alignment with user intents and improve data quality. By creating a two-stage pipeline that generates a large-scale dataset from diverse domains, the authors address the challenges of high data entropy and scarcity in training. The proposed approach is validated through a new benchmark, DataClaw_0-val, demonstrating its effectiveness in tasks like video generation and visual question answering.

zh.title: 代理数据定制：提升数据处理能力的创新
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为代理数据定制（Agentic Data Tailoring）的新范式，通过可学习的数据处理来结构化高熵的多模态数据流。我们设计了DataClaw_0-9B模型，结合监督微调（SFT）和群体相对策略优化（GRPO），在新的基准测试中实现了强大的对齐能力。该方法通过主动精炼和结构化数据，解决了现有被动注释方法的局限性，提升了数据处理的学习能力。最终，我们构建了DataClaw_0-val基准，系统量化了数据精炼能力，并在视频生成、现实世界视觉问答和图形用户界面导航等任务中验证了其有效性。

papers.title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
papers.abstract: Abstract EnterpriseClawBench presents a benchmark for enterprise agents based on real-world sessions with 852 reproducible tasks, emphasizing comprehensive evaluation metrics beyond single performance scores.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Enterprise agents increasingly operate inside workspaces: they read heterogeneous files, invoke tools, and deliver business artifacts. We introduce EnterpriseClawBench, an enterprise agent benchmark constructed from proprietary, real-world agent sessions. Starting from a large archive of workplace sessions, the EnterpriseClawBench produces 852 reproducible tasks, each paired with recovered fixtures, rewritten prompts, role classes, skill subclasses, hard rules, and semantic rubrics. Because the sessions contain internal enterprise content, we do not release the benchmark data; instead, our reusable contribution is the construction and evaluation protocol. On EnterpriseClawBench, the best configuration reaches only 0.663 (Codex with GPT-5.5). These results show that enterprise agent evaluation must report harness--model combinations, artifact delivery, visual quality, cost, runtime, and skill-transfer behavior, rather than collapsing performance into a single score. Code: https://github.com/FrontisAI/EnterpriseClawBench

ru.title: Комплексная оценка корпоративных агентов: за пределами простых метрик производительности
ru.desc: В статье представлен EnterpriseClawBench — бенчмарк для оценки enterprise agent, созданный на основе реальных рабочих сессий, содержащий 852 воспроизводимых задач. Авторы разработали методологию для конструирования и оценки агентов, работающих в корпоративной среде, где они взаимодействуют с разнородными файлами и инструментами. Ключевое предложение работы заключается в том, что метрики оценки должны включать не только процент успешных выполнений, но и характеристики доставки артефактов, визуального качества, стоимости и времени выполнения. Результаты показывают, что даже лучшие конфигурации достигают лишь 66,3% успеха, подчёркивая сложность задачи оценки корпоративных агентов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Agent Evaluation with Comprehensive Benchmarks
en.desc: EnterpriseClawBench is a new benchmark designed for evaluating enterprise agents using real-world tasks. It includes 852 reproducible tasks that assess various aspects of agent performance beyond just a single score. The benchmark focuses on how agents interact with different types of files, use tools, and produce business outputs. The evaluation metrics emphasize the importance of reporting multiple performance dimensions, such as model combinations, artifact quality, and operational efficiency.

zh.title: 企业代理评估的新标准
zh.desc: EnterpriseClawBench是一个针对企业代理的基准测试，基于852个可重复的真实工作任务，强调全面的评估指标，而不仅仅是单一的性能分数。该基准测试由Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct生成，旨在评估企业代理在工作环境中的表现，包括读取异构文件、调用工具和交付业务成果。EnterpriseClawBench的构建基于大量的工作场所会话，提供了任务、提示、角色类别、技能子类等的详细信息。评估结果表明，企业代理的评估需要考虑多种因素，而不是仅仅依赖于一个性能分数。

papers.title: Grouped Query Experts: Mixture-of-Experts on GQA Self-Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract Grouped Query Experts (GQE) improves Transformer efficiency by selectively activating query heads based on token content while maintaining key-value cache benefits of grouped-query attention.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Self-attention is central to Transformer performance and is often the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths because its pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length. Standard dense attention also applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty or information content. This uniform activation can waste compute, especially as sequences grow longer and attention cost increases rapidly. We propose Grouped Query Experts (GQE), a mixture-of-experts layer on top of grouped-query attention (GQA). Within each GQA group, a router selects k query-head experts per token while all key-value (KV) heads remain dense and unchanged. Thus, GQE keeps the KV cache benefits of GQA and reduces only the active query-head computation. On a fixed 30B token budget at the 250M parameter scale, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token.

ru.title: Избирательное внимание через экспертные маршрутизаторы
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Grouped Query Experts (GQE), который повышает эффективность Трансформеров за счёт избирательной активации головок внимания на основе содержимого токенов. Подход использует архитектуру mixture-of-experts поверх grouped-query attention, где маршрутизатор выбирает k экспертов для головок запроса каждого токена, в то время как ключи и значения остаются активными полностью. Методика позволяет сохранить преимущества кэширования ключ-значение (KV cache) при снижении вычислений для головок запроса. На бюджете в 30 млрд токенов с параметрами 250M модель достигает точности базовой GQA-модели, активируя при этом только половину головок внимания для каждого токена.

en.title: Efficient Transformers with Selective Query Activation
en.desc: Grouped Query Experts (GQE) enhances the efficiency of Transformers by activating only certain query heads based on the content of the tokens, rather than using all heads uniformly. This method addresses the high computational cost associated with self-attention, which increases quadratically with longer sequences. By implementing a mixture-of-experts approach, GQE allows for selective activation of query heads while keeping the key-value cache intact. As a result, GQE achieves comparable accuracy to traditional methods while significantly reducing the computational load by activating only half of the query heads per token.

zh.title: 选择性激活，提升 Transformer 效率
zh.desc: Grouped Query Experts (GQE) 是一种提高 Transformer 效率的方法，它通过根据令牌内容选择性地激活查询头来优化计算。传统的密集注意力机制对每个令牌应用相同的注意力头，这在长序列中会浪费计算资源。GQE 采用混合专家层，在每个分组查询注意力（GQA）组内，路由器为每个令牌选择 k 个查询头专家，同时保持键值（KV）头不变。这样，GQE 在保持 GQA 的 KV 缓存优势的同时，减少了活跃查询头的计算。

papers.title: KaLM-Reranker-V1: Fast but Not Late Interaction for Compressed Document Reranking
papers.abstract: Abstract KaLM-Reranker-V1 is a fast reranker that decouples query and passage computation using encoder-decoder architecture with Matryoshka embedding pooling and cross-attention for efficient relevance modeling.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As retrieval systems scale, high-quality reranking becomes increasingly important. However, most existing rerankers, whether encoder-based or decoder-based, jointly encode the query and passage, tightly coupling their computation and limiting deployment efficiency as well as flexibility. We present KaLM-Reranker-V1, a fast but not late-interaction (FBNL) reranker that decouples query and passage computation while retaining expressive relevance modeling. Built on an encoder-decoder architecture, KaLM-Reranker-V1 uses the encoder to pre-encode passages with Matryoshka embedding pooling, while the decoder models the system instruction, user instruction, and query intent; cross-attention then captures relevance between the query context and passage representations. This design makes KaLM-Reranker-V1 efficient through decoupled passage encoding, yet not late interaction, by preserving rich relevance modeling through cross-attention. We instantiate KaLM-Reranker-V1 in three sizes, Nano, Small, and Large, with 0.27B, 1B, and 4B activated parameters, respectively. Extensive experiments on BEIR, MIRACL, and LMEB demonstrate that KaLM-Reranker-V1 achieves strong reranking performance with superior efficiency. On BEIR, KaLM-Reranker-V1 achieves state-of-the-art performance, on par with strong industrial models such as the Qwen3-Reranker series; on MIRACL, despite not being extensively trained on multilingual data, KaLM-Reranker-V1 still shows excellent reranking performance. Moreover, on LMEB, reranking models demonstrate a clear advantage, with even the 0.27B Nano model remaining competitive with 7-12B embedding models.

ru.title: Быстрое ранжирование без компромисса в качестве
ru.desc: KaLM-Reranker-V1 — это быстрый ранжирующий модель, которая разделяет обработку запроса и документа, используя архитектуру энкодер-декодер с Matryoshka pooling и кросс-внимание. Ключевое преимущество заключается в том, что модель предварительно кодирует документы энкодером, а декодер обрабатывает запрос и намерение пользователя, что позволяет избежать совместной кодировки. Система сохраняет выразительность моделирования релевантности благодаря кросс-внимание, которое связывает представления запроса и документов. Модель доступна в трёх размерах (от 0.27B до 4B параметров) и демонстрирует лучшую производительность на бенчмарках BEIR и MIRACL, превосходя по эффективности существующие ранжирующие модели.

en.title: Decoupling for Efficient Reranking with KaLM-Reranker-V1
en.desc: KaLM-Reranker-V1 is a novel reranking model that improves the efficiency of query and passage processing by separating their computations. It utilizes an encoder-decoder architecture, where the encoder pre-encodes passages using Matryoshka embedding pooling, while the decoder focuses on understanding the query intent and system instructions. The model employs cross-attention to effectively capture the relevance between the query and passage representations, allowing for rich relevance modeling without late interaction. Extensive testing shows that KaLM-Reranker-V1 outperforms existing models in terms of efficiency and effectiveness, achieving state-of-the-art results on various benchmark datasets.

zh.title: 高效解耦的重排序模型
zh.desc: KaLM-Reranker-V1是一种快速的重排序模型，采用编码器-解码器架构，通过Matryoshka嵌入池化和交叉注意力实现高效的相关性建模。该模型将查询和文段的计算解耦，避免了传统模型中查询和文段紧密耦合的问题，从而提高了部署效率和灵活性。KaLM-Reranker-V1在多个数据集上进行了广泛的实验，显示出强大的重排序性能，尤其在BEIR数据集上达到了最先进的水平。该模型提供了多种规模的版本，确保在不同的应用场景中都能保持竞争力。

papers.title: World Action Models: A Survey
papers.abstract: Abstract World Action Models are predictive-action systems that generate future states for decision-making, with designs balancing representational richness against computational constraints.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World Action Models (WAMs) are embodied predictive-action models that make a forecast of the future available to action. Recent WAMs repurpose large video generation models, and a parallel line relies on language or vision-language backbones without a video-generation core. This rapid expansion has blurred the boundary among broad world models, video generation models, action-grounded video world models, Vision-Language-Action policies, and WAMs. This survey gives the field a common account. It first clarifies these boundaries, then organizes existing works through two complementary views. The first view asks what each method is required to generate, spanning rendered futures, latent futures, and video-generation-free action reasoning. The second view decomposes each method by predictive substrate, backbone, action coupling, and deployment regime. This anatomy supports a unified discussion of interactability, causality, persistence, physical plausibility, and generalization, followed by data, evaluation, and open challenges. Across these axes, a consistent design pattern emerges: WAMs are not simply video generators with action heads, but predictive-action methods whose design choices trade representational richness against compute, memory, latency, and action-label cost. The field is moving toward methods that generate less of the future while preserving what control requires. The survey homepage is available at https://world-action-models.github.io/.

ru.title: Предсказание будущего для управления: баланс между точностью и эффективностью
ru.desc: В статье рассматриваются World Action Models (WAM) — это предсказательные системы, которые генерируют будущие состояния окружающей среды для принятия решений агентом. Авторы проводят систематизацию существующих подходов, разделяя методы по типам генерируемых представлений (визуальные, латентные или без видео-генерации) и компонентам архитектуры (основная модель, интеграция действий, режим развёртывания). Исследование показывает, что WAM — это не просто генераторы видео с модулем действий, а специализированные модели, которые балансируют между точностью представления и вычислительными ограничениями. Ключевой вывод: развитие направлено к методам, которые генерируют только необходимую часть будущего состояния, сохраняя информацию, критичную для управления системой.

en.title: Predicting the Future: Balancing Action and Computation in World Models
en.desc: This paper discusses World Action Models (WAMs), which are systems that predict future states to aid in decision-making. WAMs balance the complexity of representation with the limitations of computational resources. The paper categorizes existing models based on their ability to generate future scenarios and their underlying architectures, including video generation and language-based approaches. It highlights the evolution of WAMs and emphasizes the need for designs that optimize performance while minimizing resource usage.

zh.title: 探索未来的行动模型
zh.desc: 世界行动模型（WAMs）是一种预测性行动系统，旨在为决策生成未来状态，同时在表示丰富性和计算约束之间取得平衡。最近的WAMs利用大型视频生成模型，并且有些方法依赖于语言或视觉-语言的基础，而不需要视频生成核心。这种快速扩展模糊了广义世界模型、视频生成模型、基于行动的视频世界模型、视觉-语言-行动策略和WAMs之间的界限。本文综述了这些模型的边界，组织了现有工作，并探讨了交互性、因果性、持久性、物理合理性和泛化等问题。

papers.title: CLI-Universe: Towards Verifiable Task Synthesis Engine for Terminal Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A principled synthesis engine generates high-quality terminal-agent tasks through multi-dimensional capability taxonomy and evidence-guided research, creating a distilled dataset that enables significant performance gains in LLM training.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct While recent LLM-based terminal agents have demonstrated promising capabilities, the scarcity of high-quality, executable training data remains a critical bottleneck. Existing synthesis pipelines typically scale by retrofitting surface-level artifacts into tasks, frequently yielding ambiguous instructions, shallow execution paths, and brittle tests that provide weak learning signals. To overcome this, we introduce CLI-Universe, a principled synthesis engine that constructs terminal-agent tasks. CLI-Universe generates candidate tasks by sampling combinations across a multi-dimensional capability taxonomy (domain, skill type, capability, and engineering pillar), then grounds each candidate through evidence-guided deep research over real-world technical materials. To ensure rigorous supervision, validated blueprints are instantiated into Dockerized environments and subjected to a multi-stage executable verification pipeline featuring rubric-gated test construction, hint-conditional filtering, and strict fail-to-pass checking. Across the full pipeline, from candidate generation to verification, approximately two-thirds of candidates are discarded, retaining only those that are genuine, verifiable, and non-trivially challenging. To validate our framework, we instantiate a highly distilled dataset of 6,000 trajectories called CLI-Universe-6K. Remarkably, fine-tuning Qwen3-32B on CLI-Universe-6K achieves 33.4% on Terminal-Bench 2.0. This sets a new state-of-the-art for models trained on open-source data at or below 32B parameters, and outperforms several models an order of magnitude larger, demonstrating the profound data efficiency of structured, high-fidelity synthesis.

ru.title: Синтез качественных данных через многомерную таксономию и проверенный отбор
ru.desc: В статье представлена система CLI-Universe для автоматического создания высококачественных обучающих данных для LLM-агентов, работающих с терминалом командной строки. Система использует многомерную таксономию навыков (домен, тип умения, возможность и инженерный компонент) для генерации кандидатов задач, а затем валидирует каждую задачу через анализ реальных технических материалов. Строгая верификация включает несколько этапов: построение тестов по рубрикам, фильтрацию с условными подсказками и проверку прохождения тестов, при этом две трети кандидатов отбраковываются. Результаты показывают, что fine-tuning модели Qwen3-32B на отобранном датасете из 6000 примеров достигает новых показателей качества и превосходит модели, которые значительно больше по размеру.

en.title: Revolutionizing Task Generation for Terminal Agents with CLI-Universe
en.desc: This paper presents CLI-Universe, a synthesis engine designed to create high-quality tasks for terminal agents by utilizing a multi-dimensional capability taxonomy. It addresses the challenge of limited executable training data in large language models (LLMs) by generating a distilled dataset through evidence-guided research and rigorous validation processes. The synthesis pipeline ensures that only the most relevant and challenging tasks are retained, significantly improving the learning signals for LLM training. The results show that fine-tuning the Qwen3-32B model on the newly created CLI-Universe-6K dataset leads to state-of-the-art performance, highlighting the effectiveness of structured data synthesis in enhancing model capabilities.

zh.title: 高效合成，提升终端代理任务质量
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为CLI-Universe的合成引擎，能够通过多维能力分类法生成高质量的终端代理任务。这种方法通过对真实技术材料的证据引导研究，确保生成的任务具有可执行性和挑战性。CLI-Universe的生成过程经过严格的验证管道，确保保留的任务是真实且可验证的。通过在CLI-Universe-6K数据集上微调Qwen3-32B模型，取得了显著的性能提升，展示了高保真合成的卓越数据效率。

papers.title: EvoEmbedding: Evolvable Representations for Long-Context Retrieval and Agentic Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract EvoEmbedding is a dynamic embedding model that generates adaptive representations by maintaining a continuously updated latent memory, enabling improved retrieval performance in long-context scenarios.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Existing embedding models are inherently static: they encode text segments in isolation, ignoring their surrounding context and temporal order. This paper introduces EvoEmbedding, a novel embedding model that generates evolvable representations for retrieval. It is tailored for long-context scenarios, where information is dynamic, sequential, and requires continuous state tracking. Our design is simple: EvoEmbedding maintains a continuously updated latent memory as it sequentially processes inputs, and uses it alongside the raw content to jointly generate evolvable embeddings. Consequently, for the same query, our model adapts its representation to retrieve distinct targets based on the evolving context, going beyond static semantic search. To equip the model with this capability, we construct EvoTrain-180K, a diverse dataset for the joint optimization of latent memory and retrieval. Furthermore, we introduce a memory queue to prevent representation collapse during recurrent encoding, alongside segment-batching techniques that tackle significant length variance and accelerate training by 3.8times. Extensive experiments show that our model not only outperforms larger-scale specialists (e.g., Qwen3-Embedding-8B and KaLM-Embedding-Gemma3-12B) across a range of long-context retrieval benchmarks, but also generalizes well to downstream tasks (e.g., personalization) with contexts 10times longer than its training window. Notably, EvoEmbedding seamlessly integrates into agentic workflows to boost performance. For instance, a naive RAG pipeline equipped with our model surpasses dedicated agentic memory systems. Project Page: https://clare-nie.github.io/EvoEmbedding.

ru.title: Эмбеддинги, которые эволюционируют вместе с контекстом
ru.desc: EvoEmbedding представляет собой динамическую модель эмбеддингов, которая генерирует адаптивные представления текста путём поддержания постоянно обновляемой скрытой памяти. В отличие от традиционных статических моделей, EvoEmbedding обрабатывает входные данные последовательно, учитывая контекст и временной порядок информации. Модель изменяет представление одного и того же запроса в зависимости от развивающегося контекста, выходя за границы статического семантического поиска. Авторы представили специализированный датасет EvoTrain-180K и техники оптимизации, позволяющие модели превосходить более крупные специализированные модели на задачах поиска в длинном контексте.

en.title: EvoEmbedding: Dynamic Contextual Retrieval Redefined
en.desc: EvoEmbedding is a new dynamic embedding model designed to improve retrieval performance in situations with long and changing contexts. Unlike traditional static embedding models that treat text segments independently, EvoEmbedding continuously updates its latent memory while processing inputs sequentially. This allows the model to adapt its representations based on the evolving context, enabling it to retrieve different targets for the same query. The model is trained on a diverse dataset and incorporates techniques to prevent representation collapse, resulting in faster training and better performance on long-context retrieval tasks.

zh.title: 动态嵌入，智能检索新体验
zh.desc: EvoEmbedding是一种动态嵌入模型，通过维护一个持续更新的潜在记忆来生成自适应表示，从而在长上下文场景中提高检索性能。与传统的静态嵌入模型不同，EvoEmbedding能够根据不断变化的上下文调整其表示，以检索不同的目标。该模型的设计简单，通过顺序处理输入并结合原始内容生成可演变的嵌入。实验表明，EvoEmbedding在长上下文检索基准测试中表现优于其他大型模型，并且能够很好地推广到下游任务。

papers.title: BioMatrix: Towards a Comprehensive Biological Foundation Model Spanning the Modality Matrix of Sequences, Structures, and Language
papers.abstract: Abstract BioMatrix is a novel multimodal foundation model that integrates molecular sequences, structures, and natural language into a unified decoder-only architecture for diverse biological tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present BioMatrix, the first multimodal foundation model that natively integrates sequences, structures, and natural language for both molecules and proteins within a single decoder-only architecture. Existing biological foundation models pursue native multimodality and broad entity coverage separately: those that fuse multiple modalities under a shared objective remain confined to a single entity type, while those spanning multiple entity types either omit explicit structural modeling or rely on adapter-based designs in which the model cannot natively generate the very modalities it can read. BioMatrix closes this gap by mapping molecular sequences (supporting both SMILES and SELFIES notations), molecular structures, protein sequences, protein structures, and natural language into a shared discrete token space through a unified tokenization scheme, so that all modalities are consumed and produced uniformly under a single next-token prediction objective -- without external encoders, projection adapters, or modality-specific output heads. Built upon the Qwen3 language model (1.7B and 4B), BioMatrix is continually pretrained on 304.4 billion tokens spanning general and domain-specific text, sequence and structure views of molecules and proteins, and cross-modal corpora that interleave biomolecular entities with scientific text and link distinct entities through molecule-protein and protein-protein interaction data. After tuning on a comprehensive suite of downstream applications covering 80 tasks across 6 categories -- encompassing single-entity and multi-entity understanding and generation tasks across and within modalities -- BioMatrix achieves state-of-the-art or competitive performance on 77 out of 80 tasks, demonstrating that a single, natively multimodal generalist model can effectively match or surpass specialized approaches across a wide range of biological tasks.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China
School of Artificial Intelligence, Wuhan University

ru.title: Единая мультимодальная модель для всех молекулярных и белковых задач
ru.desc: BioMatrix — это первая мультимодальная foundation модель, которая объединяет молекулярные последовательности, структуры и естественный язык в единую decoder-only архитектуру для работы с молекулами и белками. Модель использует унифицированную схему токенизации, которая преобразует все типы данных в общее дискретное пространство токенов, позволяя одинаково обрабатывать и генерировать все модальности через предсказание следующего токена. BioMatrix была предварительно обучена на 304.4 миллиардах токенов, включая текст, последовательности и структуры биомолекул, а также кросс-модальные корпусы с данными о взаимодействиях молекул и белков. Модель достигает лучших или конкурентных результатов на 77 из 80 задач различных категорий, демонстрируя, что единая мультимодальная модель может эффективно заменить специализированные подходы в биологических приложениях.

en.title: BioMatrix: Unifying Biology with Multimodal Mastery
en.desc: BioMatrix is a groundbreaking multimodal foundation model designed to handle various biological tasks by integrating molecular sequences, structures, and natural language. It uniquely employs a decoder-only architecture that allows it to process and generate different types of biological data, including proteins and molecules, without needing separate encoders or adapters. By utilizing a unified tokenization scheme, BioMatrix can seamlessly manage multiple modalities, achieving impressive results across 80 diverse tasks. This model demonstrates that a single, comprehensive approach can outperform specialized models in understanding and generating biological information.

zh.title: BioMatrix：生物任务的多模态通用模型
zh.desc: BioMatrix是一种新型的多模态基础模型，能够将分子序列、结构和自然语言整合到一个统一的解码器架构中，适用于多种生物任务。它通过统一的标记化方案，将分子序列、分子结构、蛋白质序列、蛋白质结构和自然语言映射到共享的离散标记空间中。BioMatrix在3044亿个标记上进行持续预训练，涵盖了广泛的文本、序列和结构视图，展示了其在80个任务中的优越性能。该模型证明了一个单一的多模态通用模型能够有效地匹配或超越专门化的方法。

papers.title: HydraHead: From Head-Level Functional Heterogeneity to Specialized Attention Hybridization
papers.abstract: Abstract HydraHead is a novel attention hybridization architecture that combines Full Attention and Linear Attention at the head level, achieving superior long-context performance with reduced training overhead through interpretability-driven selection and scale-normalized fusion.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The quadratic complexity of attention poses a critical bottleneck for long-context processing, spurring interest in hybrid attention designs. Most open-source hybrid models adopt a layer-wise strategy. Yet, prior work has noted the inherent difficulty of integrating Linear Attention (LA) with Full Attention (FA), suggesting that the design space of attention hybridization remains underexplored. To probe this space, we conduct interpretability analysis and observe that layers exhibit block-wise functional similarity, while individual heads within the same layer display distinct functional specialization despite sharing input features. This head-level heterogeneity suggests that the head dimension provides a natural and principled granularity for fusing heterogeneous attention signals. Building on this insight, we introduce HydraHead, a novel architecture that hybridizes FA and LA along the head axis. HydraHead features two key innovations: (1) an interpretability-driven selection strategy that identifies retrieval-critical heads and preserves FA only for them, and (2) a scale-normalized fusion module that reconciles the distributional gap between FA and LA head outputs. By leveraging a three-stage transfer pipeline with parameter reuse and distillation, we achieve high-performance hybrid models with minimal training overhead. Under a unified training setup, HydraHead outperforms other hybrid designs in long-context tasks while maintaining strong general reasoning. With interpretability-driven head selection, it matches a 3:1 layer-wise hybrid's long-context performance at a 7:1 LA-to-FA ratio. Crucially, trained on only 15B tokens, HydraHead achieves over 69% improvement over the baseline at 512K context length, approaching Qwen3.5, a leading model of comparable size with a native context length of 256K. This highlights the significant scaling potential of head-level hybridization.

ru.title: Гибридизация внимания на уровне голов для эффективной обработки длинных контекстов
ru.desc: HydraHead — это новая архитектура внимания, которая комбинирует полное внимание и линейное внимание на уровне отдельных голов внимания, достигая превосходной производительности на длинных контекстах с минимальными затратами на обучение. Авторы провели анализ интерпретируемости и обнаружили, что различные головы внимания выполняют специализированные функции, несмотря на общие входные данные, что подтвердило целесообразность гибридизации именно на уровне голов. Архитектура включает две ключевые инновации: стратегию выбора голов, критичных для извлечения информации, и модуль нормализованного слияния для согласования распределений выходов полного и линейного внимания. При обучении на 15 миллиардах токенов HydraHead достигает улучшения более чем на 69% на контексте длиной 512K и демонстрирует значительный потенциал масштабирования гибридного внимания на уровне голов.

en.title: HydraHead: Revolutionizing Attention with Head-Level Hybridization
en.desc: HydraHead is a new architecture that combines Full Attention (FA) and Linear Attention (LA) at the head level to improve performance on long-context tasks while reducing training costs. It uses an interpretability-driven approach to select which heads should use FA, ensuring that only the most critical heads retain this more complex attention mechanism. Additionally, it incorporates a scale-normalized fusion module to effectively merge outputs from FA and LA heads, addressing their differences. This innovative design allows HydraHead to outperform existing hybrid models, achieving significant improvements in long-context processing with fewer training resources.

zh.title: 头部级别混合，提升长上下文性能！
zh.desc: HydraHead是一种新颖的注意力混合架构，它在头部级别结合了全注意力和线性注意力，显著提高了长上下文处理的性能，同时减少了训练开销。通过可解释性驱动的选择策略，HydraHead能够识别出关键的头部，并仅为这些头部保留全注意力。该架构还引入了一个规模归一化的融合模块，以调和全注意力和线性注意力输出之间的分布差异。经过三阶段的迁移学习，HydraHead在长上下文任务中表现优异，显示出头部级别混合的巨大潜力。

papers.title: SkillHarness: Harnessing Safe Skills for Computer-Use Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SkillHarness is a framework that enables computer-use agents to safely learn and execute skills in dynamic environments by incorporating safety constraints and adaptive skill selection mechanisms.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) are increasingly deployed in dynamic interactive environments, creating a growing need for continual skill learning during interaction. Recent approaches address this challenge by learning reusable skills from successful trajectories. However, these skill learning methods largely assume static and safe environments, overlooking risks from adversarial interactions (e.g., prompt injections) and environmental dynamics (e.g., pop-ups). In dynamic settings, such assumptions can lead to risky skill learning and brittle execution, undermining the reliability of CUAs. This raises the question: how can CUAs learn and use skills safely in dynamic environments? To address this problem, we propose SkillHarness, a framework for safe skill harnessing in dynamic environments. SkillHarness moves beyond static skill abstractions by modeling skill learning and utilization as a safety-constrained interaction process. Specifically, we introduce the skill boundary that leverages multi-source supervision signals to identify safe skills from interaction trajectories, and construct self-improving safety constraints throughout the skill lifecycle. In addition, SkillHarness introduces selective skill reuse, where tasks are guided to decompose according to context and completed through the selective activation of skill subsets. Our experiments demonstrate that SkillHarness significantly reduces the unsafe rate of learned skills by 57.1% and consistently improves execution stability under dynamic environmental changes, outperforming existing baselines.

ru.title: Безопасное обучение и адаптивное применение навыков в динамических средах
ru.desc: SkillHarness — это фреймворк, который позволяет агентам, работающим с компьютером, безопасно обучаться и выполнять навыки в динамических окружениях. Система использует границы навыков на основе сигналов с несколько источников для выявления безопасных навыков из траекторий взаимодействия и постоянно улучшает ограничения безопасности на всех этапах жизненного цикла навыка. Фреймворк вводит селективное переиспользование навыков, позволяя агентам разлагать задачи в соответствии с контекстом и выполнять их через выборочную активацию подмножества навыков. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SkillHarness снижает процент небезопасных обученных навыков на 57,1% и значительно повышает стабильность выполнения в условиях динамических изменений окружения.

en.title: Safe Skill Learning in Dynamic Environments
en.desc: SkillHarness is a framework designed to help computer-use agents (CUAs) learn and perform skills safely in changing environments. It addresses the limitations of traditional skill learning methods that assume static conditions, which can lead to unsafe outcomes during interactions. By introducing concepts like skill boundaries and selective skill reuse, SkillHarness ensures that CUAs can adaptively select and execute skills while adhering to safety constraints. Experimental results show that this framework significantly reduces the risk of unsafe skill execution and enhances stability in dynamic settings.

zh.title: 动态环境中的安全技能学习框架
zh.desc: SkillHarness是一个框架，旨在使计算机使用代理在动态环境中安全地学习和执行技能。它通过引入安全约束和自适应技能选择机制，解决了在互动过程中持续学习技能的需求。该框架通过建模技能学习和利用为一个安全约束的互动过程，确保在动态环境中识别安全技能。实验结果表明，SkillHarness显著降低了学习技能的不安全率，并在动态环境变化下提高了执行的稳定性。

papers.title: Learning from Your Own Mistakes: Constructing Learnable Micro-Reflective Trajectories for Self-Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO) enhances large language model reasoning by creating explicit corrective trajectories that preserve erroneous reasoning while incorporating natural-language diagnoses and corrections, outperforming traditional self-distillation methods through improved error-correction capabilities.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Self-distillation improves reasoning in large language models by using the model's own rollouts as training signal, typically through implicit logit-level alignment that minimizes KL divergence toward a privileged target distribution. However, because this supervision is generated via uncontrolled sampling, it provides no diagnostic insight into the model's specific errors or corrective guidance for its individual failure patterns. Consequently, the model learns to imitate a privileged distribution rather than receiving fine-grained corrections that pinpoint where and why its reasoning fails. In this paper, we propose Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO), which advances self-distillation from implicit distributional alignment to explicit trajectory construction. During RL training, the model produces both correct and incorrect rollouts to the same query, and TAPO leverages this contrastive structure to construct micro-reflective corrections, new training trajectories that retain the model's erroneous reasoning up to the point of failure, then insert a natural-language diagnosis and corrected reasoning guided by a correct reference from the same sampling group. Since each trajectory is anchored in the learner's own prefix and solutions, the corrective signal preserves the model's on-policy distribution to a greater extent than the position-wise alignment imposed by KL-based methods. To integrate these trajectories, TAPO introduces difficulty-aware candidate selection at the model's capability boundary and decoupled advantage estimation to prevent gradient contamination. Experiments on AIME 2024, AIME 2025, and HMMT 2025 show that TAPO achieves consistent improvements over GRPO under the same number of training steps. Further analysis demonstrates that TAPO strengthens both first-pass reasoning and error-correction effectiveness.

ru.title: Обучение через явную коррекцию ошибок в рассуждениях языковых моделей
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод TAPO, который улучшает рассуждения больших языковых моделей посредством создания явных корректирующих траекторий. Вместо традиционной самодистилляции, которая использует неявное выравнивание на уровне логитов, TAPO сохраняет ошибочное рассуждение модели и дополняет его диагностикой на естественном языке с исправленным решением. Метод использует контрастную структуру, сравнивая правильные и неправильные выводы, чтобы создать обучающие примеры, сохраняющие распределение модели лучше, чем KL-базированные подходы. Экспериментальная оценка на математических олимпиадах показывает, что TAPO демонстрирует стабильные улучшения в сравнении с базовыми методами при одинаковом количестве шагов обучения.

en.title: Enhancing Reasoning with Corrective Trajectories in TAPO
en.desc: Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO) is a novel approach that enhances the reasoning capabilities of large language models by explicitly creating corrective trajectories. Unlike traditional self-distillation methods that rely on implicit logit-level alignment, TAPO retains the model's erroneous reasoning while integrating natural-language diagnoses and corrections. This method allows the model to learn from its mistakes by generating both correct and incorrect responses, leading to more effective error correction. Experiments show that TAPO significantly outperforms existing methods, improving both initial reasoning and the ability to correct errors.

zh.title: 轨迹增强，提升推理能力！
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为轨迹增强策略优化（TAPO）的方法，旨在提升大型语言模型的推理能力。TAPO通过构建明确的纠正轨迹，保留错误推理的同时，加入自然语言的诊断和修正，从而超越传统的自蒸馏方法。与隐式对齐方法不同，TAPO利用模型生成的正确和错误的推理结果，构建微观反思的纠正信号。实验结果表明，TAPO在多个基准测试中均表现出一致的性能提升，增强了模型的推理和错误修正能力。

papers.title: Deeper is Not Always Better: Mitigating the Alignment Tax via Confident Layer Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract Autoregressive generation in large language models traditionally uses the final layer for token prediction, but a new decoding strategy dynamically selects more reliable intermediate layers based on entropy-guided search, improving reasoning performance with minimal computational overhead.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Autoregressive generation in large language models (LLMs) conventionally decodes from the final layer, assuming that deeper representations yield more reliable next-token predictions. We revisit this assumption by revealing a recurring Guess-Refine-Perturb dynamic: early layers form coarse guesses, intermediate layers refine reasoning-relevant semantics, and final layers can perturb these refined predictions toward generic or alignment-preferred tokens. We introduce Confident Decoding, a training-free decoding strategy that dynamically selects the most reliable near-final layer through entropy-guided conservative backward search. We further provide a theoretical formulation of layer selection as an optimal stopping problem, showing that under bounded projection noise and dominant late-stage alignment perturbation, our search rule filters perturbation while bounding the loss relative to the oracle refinement layer. Experiments across dense and Mixture-of-Experts LLMs demonstrate consistent gains on challenging reasoning benchmarks, including GPQA-Diamond, Omni-MATH, and HLE, with zero memory overhead and less than 2% latency increase. These results suggest dynamically bypassing final-layer perturbations can unlock stronger reasoning behavior from aligned LLMs.

ru.title: Пропуск финального слоя: поиск оптимального уровня для лучшего рассуждения в LLM
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что глубокие слои нейронных сетей большие языковые модели не всегда дают лучшие предсказания токенов. Авторы предлагают метод «Confident Decoding» — стратегию декодирования без обучения, которая динамически выбирает наиболее надёжный промежуточный слой на основе анализа энтропии. Теоретически задача выбора слоя формулируется как проблема оптимальной остановки, что позволяет отфильтровать артефакты финального слоя при минимальных потерях качества. Эксперименты демонстрируют улучшение на задачах логического рассуждения без увеличения памяти и с минимальной задержкой в вычислениях.

en.title: Unlocking Better Reasoning in LLMs with Layer Selection
en.desc: This paper introduces a new decoding strategy called Confident Decoding for autoregressive generation in large language models (LLMs). Instead of relying solely on the final layer for token predictions, it dynamically selects more reliable intermediate layers based on an entropy-guided search. The authors reveal a process where early layers make rough guesses, intermediate layers refine these guesses, and final layers may introduce unwanted changes. Their approach shows improved reasoning performance on various benchmarks with minimal computational costs, suggesting that avoiding final-layer perturbations can enhance the reasoning capabilities of LLMs.

zh.title: 动态选择中间层，提升推理性能
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型中的自回归生成方法，提出了一种新的解码策略。该策略通过熵引导搜索动态选择更可靠的中间层，以提高推理性能，同时保持较低的计算开销。研究表明，早期层形成粗略猜测，中间层细化语义，而最终层可能会扰动这些细化的预测。通过引入自信解码，论文展示了如何在不增加内存负担的情况下，优化推理过程。

papers.title: Unlimited OCR Works
papers.abstract: Abstract Unlimited OCR introduces Reference Sliding Window Attention to eliminate growing memory consumption during long-sequence OCR tasks, enabling efficient transcription of multiple pages in a single forward pass.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recently, end-to-end OCR models, exemplified by DeepSeek OCR, have once again thrust OCR into the spotlight. A widely held view is that employing a large language model (LLM) as the decoder allows the model to leverage the prior distribution of language, leading to improved OCR performance. However, the downside is equally evident: as the output sequence lengthens, the accumulated KV cache drives up memory consumption and progressively slows down generation. This stands in stark contrast to humans, who exhibit no such decline in efficiency during long-horizon copying tasks. In this technical report, we propose Unlimited OCR, a model designed to emulate human parsing working memory. Taking DeepSeek OCR as the baseline, we replace all attention layers in the decoder with our proposed Reference Sliding Window Attention (R-SWA), which reduces attention computation costs while maintaining a constant KV cache throughout the entire decoding process. By combining the high compression rate of DeepSeek OCR's encoder with our constant KV cache design, Unlimited OCR can transcribe dozens of pages of documents in a single forward pass under a standard maximum length of 32K. More importantly, R-SWA is a general-purpose parsing attention mechanism - beyond OCR, it is equally applicable to tasks such as ASR, translation, etc. Codes and model weights are publicly available at http://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR.

ru.title: Безлимитное распознавание текста с постоянной памятью
ru.desc: В работе представлена архитектура Unlimited OCR, которая решает проблему растущего потребления памяти при обработке длинных последовательностей в задачах оптического распознавания текста. Авторы заменили стандартные слои внимания в декодере на механизм Reference Sliding Window Attention (R-SWA), который поддерживает константный размер KV cache на протяжении всего процесса декодирования. Это позволяет модели обрабатывать документы из десятков страниц в один проход, имитируя работу человеческой рабочей памяти при распознавании текста. Предложенный механизм R-SWA является универсальным и может быть применён к другим задачам, таким как распознавание речи и машинный перевод.

en.title: Efficient Long-Sequence OCR with Reference Sliding Window Attention
en.desc: Unlimited OCR introduces a new method called Reference Sliding Window Attention (R-SWA) to improve the efficiency of Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for long documents. This method reduces memory usage by maintaining a constant key-value (KV) cache, allowing the model to process multiple pages in one go without slowing down. By using R-SWA in place of traditional attention layers, the model can handle longer sequences while still leveraging the strengths of large language models. This approach not only enhances OCR performance but is also adaptable for other tasks like automatic speech recognition (ASR) and translation.

zh.title: 无限OCR：高效长序列转录的新方法
zh.desc: Unlimited OCR提出了一种参考滑动窗口注意力机制，以解决长序列OCR任务中不断增长的内存消耗问题。这种方法使得在一次前向传递中高效地转录多页文档成为可能。通过将DeepSeek OCR的解码器中的所有注意力层替换为参考滑动窗口注意力，Unlimited OCR在保持恒定的KV缓存的同时，降低了注意力计算成本。R-SWA不仅适用于OCR任务，还可以广泛应用于语音识别、翻译等其他任务。

papers.title: Deep Research in Physical Sciences: A Multi-Agent Framework and Comprehensive Benchmark
papers.abstract: Abstract PhySciBench benchmark reveals limited performance of current LLM agents in physical science research, leading to development of DelveAgent framework that improves accuracy through modular design and physics-grounded mechanisms.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Deep research agents are Large Language Model (LLM)-based systems designed for autonomous, multi-step scientific reasoning, and they hold immense potential for accelerating research in the physical sciences. However, comprehensive and in-depth evaluations of their capabilities within this domain remain lacking. To address this gap, we introduce PhySciBench, a benchmark highly relevant to physical science research, comprising 200 expert-curated questions, balanced between physics and chemistry, across six task categories that reflect real-world scientific workflows. Evaluations of state-of-the-art models and agent systems on PhySciBench reveal limited performance; even the strongest baseline, Gemini Deep Research, achieves an accuracy of only 33.5%. Analysis of failure cases identifies three recurrent deficiencies: fragility in extended reasoning chains, limited knowledge transfer across steps, and a lack of physics-grounded self-verification. Motivated by these findings, we develop DelveAgent, a modular multi-agent framework equipped with an adaptive planning loop, dual-granularity memory, and a hierarchical physics-grounded reflection mechanism. Across four scientific benchmarks, DelveAgent improves accuracy by up to 7.5 percentage points while reducing inference costs to approximately one-third of the strongest baseline. These results establish the significance of PhySciBench as a critical benchmark for evaluating AI systems in the physical sciences and demonstrate that architectural specialization can effectively enhance the reliability of autonomous scientific research.
affiliations: Beijing National Laboratory for Condensed Matter
Condensed Matter Physics Data Center, Chinese Academy
Physics and Institute of Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Специализированная архитектура для надёжного автономного научного исследования
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк PhySciBench для оценки способностей LLM-агентов в физических науках, содержащий 200 экспертных вопросов по физике и химии. Анализ показал, что современные модели достигают низкой точности (33.5%), страдая от хрупкости в длинных цепочках рассуждений и отсутствия физически обоснованной верификации. Авторы разработали DelveAgent — модульный мультиагентный фреймворк с адаптивным планированием, двухуровневой памятью и иерархическим механизмом рефлексии. Предложенный подход улучшает точность на 7.5 процентных пункта при снижении вычислительных затрат до трети от базовой модели.

en.title: Enhancing LLMs for Scientific Research with DelveAgent
en.desc: The paper introduces PhySciBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the performance of Large Language Model (LLM) agents in physical science research. It highlights the limitations of current models, such as Gemini Deep Research, which only achieved 33.5% accuracy on complex scientific tasks. To improve performance, the authors developed DelveAgent, a modular framework that incorporates adaptive planning and physics-based verification mechanisms. DelveAgent shows a notable increase in accuracy and a significant reduction in inference costs, demonstrating the importance of specialized architectures in enhancing AI's role in scientific research.

zh.title: 提升物理科学研究的智能代理框架
zh.desc: PhySciBench基准测试揭示了当前大型语言模型（LLM）在物理科学研究中的性能有限，因此开发了DelveAgent框架，通过模块化设计和基于物理的机制提高准确性。生成的深度研究代理是基于LLM的系统，旨在实现自主的多步骤科学推理，具有加速物理科学研究的巨大潜力。然而，对这些系统在该领域能力的全面评估仍然缺乏。DelveAgent在四个科学基准测试中提高了准确性，并显著降低了推理成本，证明了架构专业化可以有效增强自主科学研究的可靠性。

papers.title: Foresight: Failure Detection for Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation with Action-Conditioned World Model Latents
papers.abstract: Abstract A failure detection framework for long-horizon robotic tasks uses action-conditioned world models and functional conformal prediction to monitor manipulation trajectories with only final task labels.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long-horizon tasks are common in real-world robotic deployments, yet failure detection for such tasks remains underexplored. Detecting failures in long-horizon robotic tasks is particularly challenging because failure onset is often ambiguous and dense temporal annotations are typically unavailable. We present Foresight, a failure detection framework that monitors manipulation trajectories using latent representations from an action-conditioned world model. Foresight is trained using only final task-level success or failure labels. By leveraging predictive world-model embeddings, our method provides a unified framework for failure detection across different policies. We further use functional conformal prediction (FCP) to calibrate detection thresholds adaptively. We evaluate Foresight with state-of-the-art vision-language-action policies in simulation on LIBERO-Long, ManiSkill-Long, and BEHAVIOR-1K, compare it against state-of-the-artfailure detection methods, and validate it on real robots with three long-horizon tasks on a ReactorX-200 arm and one task on a Franka arm. Our results suggest that action-conditioned world-model embeddings provide a scalable representation for reliable failure monitoring in long-horizon manipulation.

ru.title: Обнаружение отказов в долгоразмерных робототехнических задачах через скрытые представления мировых моделей
ru.desc: В работе предложена система обнаружения сбоев Foresight для долгоразмерных задач манипуляции роботами, которая использует действие-обусловленные мировые модели для мониторинга траекторий. Система обучается только на финальных метках успеха или неудачи задачи, без требования плотных временных аннотаций. Для адаптивной калибровки порогов обнаружения применяется функциональное конформное предсказание. Метод показал высокую эффективность на различных политиках и успешно валидирован как в симуляции (LIBERO-Long, ManiSkill-Long, BEHAVIOR-1K), так и на реальных роботических манипуляторах.

en.title: Foresight: Smart Failure Detection for Robots in Long Tasks
en.desc: This paper introduces Foresight, a framework designed to detect failures in long-horizon robotic tasks using action-conditioned world models. It addresses the challenge of ambiguous failure onset and the lack of dense temporal annotations by relying solely on final task success or failure labels for training. Foresight utilizes latent representations from a predictive world model to monitor manipulation trajectories, allowing for a unified approach to failure detection across various policies. Additionally, it employs functional conformal prediction to adaptively calibrate detection thresholds, enhancing the reliability of failure monitoring in real-world robotic applications.

zh.title: Foresight：长时间任务的智能故障检测
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种用于长时间机器人任务的故障检测框架，名为Foresight。该框架利用基于动作的世界模型的潜在表示来监控操作轨迹，仅依赖最终的任务成功或失败标签进行训练。通过使用功能性符合预测（FCP），Foresight能够自适应地校准检测阈值，从而提高故障检测的准确性。实验结果表明，基于动作的世界模型嵌入为长时间操作提供了可扩展的可靠故障监控表示。

papers.title: Self-Compacting Language Model Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SelfCompact is a scaffolding approach that enables models to autonomously determine optimal compaction timing and methods for managing long agent traces, achieving better performance with reduced token costs compared to fixed-interval methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long agent traces composed of chains of thought and tool calls accumulate stale content that anchor subsequent generations, and eventually outgrow the context window. Existing scaffolds mitigate it with fixed-interval compaction triggered at a token threshold. Such triggers pay no heed to trajectory structure, risking discard of partial results mid-derivation or mid-search. We propose SelfCompact, a scaffold that allows the model itself to decide when and how to compact. Specifically, it pairs two inference-time elements: (i) a compaction tool the model invokes to summarize the accumulated context, and (ii) a lightweight rubric specifying when to fire (a sub-task has resolved, or the trajectory is converging) and when to suppress (mid-derivation, or when stuck). Both are needed. The tool alone is unevenly used across open-weight models, often invoked at unhelpful moments or not at all; the rubric alone cannot act. Together, they elicit effective adaptive compaction without any fine-tuning or external supervision. We present empirical results on six benchmarks (competitive math and agentic search) and seven models. Our results show that SelfCompact matches or exceeds fixed-interval summarization at a fraction of the token cost, improving over a no-summarization baseline by up to 18.1 points on math and 5-9 points on agentic search at 30-70% lower per-question cost. Our results expose a meta-cognitive gap: although unprompted models cannot reliably tell when their own context is rotting, a lightweight rubric closes this gap, reframing when to compact as a capability that scaffolds can supply without training.

ru.title: Модель сама решает, когда сжимать контекст
ru.desc: SelfCompact — это метод scaffolding, который позволяет моделям самостоятельно определять оптимальное время и способ сжатия длинных трассировок агента. Длинные последовательности действий и цепочки рассуждений накапливают устаревшую информацию, которая мешает генерации и может превысить размер контекстного окна. Существующие подходы используют фиксированный интервал сжатия по количеству токенов, но это не учитывает структуру траектории и может удалить промежуточные результаты. Предложенный метод сочетает инструмент сжатия, который модель может вызывать самостоятельно, с лёгким набором правил для определения оптимального момента компактизации, достигая улучшения качества на 18% при снижении затрат токенов на 30-70% без дополнительного обучения.

en.title: Empowering Models with Adaptive Compaction: SelfCompact
en.desc: SelfCompact is a novel approach in machine learning that allows models to autonomously decide when to compact their long agent traces, which consist of sequences of thoughts and tool calls. This method improves performance by reducing token costs compared to traditional fixed-interval compaction methods. By integrating a compaction tool and a lightweight rubric, SelfCompact enables models to summarize accumulated context effectively without requiring fine-tuning or external supervision. Empirical results demonstrate that SelfCompact outperforms fixed-interval summarization while significantly lowering costs, highlighting the importance of adaptive compaction in enhancing model efficiency.

zh.title: 自适应压缩：模型自主优化上下文管理
zh.desc: SelfCompact是一种支架方法，使模型能够自主决定最佳的压缩时机和管理长代理轨迹的方法。与固定间隔方法相比，它在减少令牌成本的同时实现了更好的性能。该方法结合了两个推理时的元素：一个用于总结累积上下文的压缩工具和一个轻量级的标准，用于指定何时触发压缩。实验结果表明，SelfCompact在多个基准测试中表现优于固定间隔总结，且每个问题的成本降低了30-70%。

papers.title: Training Open Models for Agentic Phone Use
papers.abstract: Abstract PhoneBuddy combines real and mock app environments to improve training of open models for phone use, demonstrating enhanced task success rates through mixed reinforcement learning approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Phones are becoming an important execution surface for general-purpose agents, but training open models for reliable phone use remains difficult because the environment that matters at deployment, real devices running real apps, is slow, stateful, side-effectful, and hard to reset or verify, while scalable mock environments only approximate real behavior. We present PhoneBuddy, a training recipe and open-model line for agentic phone use that combines a real-app environment with a mock-app environment, PhoneWorld, which reconstructs runnable mock apps from real GUI usage structure. PhoneBuddy first builds a shared supervised fine-tuning stage from trajectories collected in both environments, then compares real-app RL against mixed RL across both environments. Across a 150-task human evaluation on real phones spanning apps, mini-apps, and cross-app workflows, task success rate improves from 36.67\% after supervised fine-tuning to 40.67\% after real-app RL and 45.33\% after mixed RL. On AndroidWorld, the same progression rises from 60.3\% to 77.2\% to 83.2\%. These results show that mock-app training is not a replacement for real-app RL, but a complementary source of scalable, resettable, and automatically checked interaction. The gains are strongest on app and mini-app tasks, while long-horizontal cross-app workflows remain an important open challenge.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China
The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

ru.title: Синтетические симуляции как дополнение к реальному окружению в обучении мобильных агентов
ru.desc: В работе представлен PhoneBuddy — метод обучения открытых моделей для управления смартфонами, который комбинирует реальное окружение с мок-приложениями для улучшения надёжности агентов. Авторы разработали синтетическое окружение PhoneWorld, восстанавливающее рабочие мок-приложения из реальной структуры интерфейса. Обучение проводится в два этапа: сначала supervised fine-tuning на данных из обоих окружений, затем reinforcement learning — сначала только на реальных приложениях, потом смешанный RL на обоих окружениях. Результаты показывают, что комбинированный подход значительно превосходит чистый real-app RL, повышая успешность выполнения задач с 36.67% до 45.33% на реальных телефонах и с 60.3% до 83.2% на тестовом наборе.

en.title: Enhancing Phone App Training with Real and Mock Environments
en.desc: The paper introduces PhoneBuddy, a novel approach that enhances the training of open models for phone applications by integrating both real and mock app environments. It utilizes mixed reinforcement learning techniques to improve task success rates, demonstrating that training in a mock environment can complement real-app training. The study shows significant improvements in task success rates across various evaluations, indicating that while mock environments cannot replace real interactions, they provide valuable scalable training opportunities. The findings highlight the effectiveness of combining supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning to optimize agent performance in complex app workflows.

zh.title: PhoneBuddy：提升手机使用的智能训练方法
zh.desc: PhoneBuddy 是一种结合真实和模拟应用环境的训练方法，旨在提高开放模型在手机使用中的表现。通过混合强化学习方法，PhoneBuddy 显著提高了任务成功率，尤其是在应用和迷你应用任务上。该方法首先在真实和模拟环境中收集轨迹进行监督微调，然后比较真实应用的强化学习与混合强化学习的效果。研究结果表明，模拟应用训练并不能替代真实应用的强化学习，而是提供了一种可扩展、可重置和自动检查的交互方式。

papers.title: Notes2Skills: From Lab Notebooks to Certainty-Aware Scientific Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract Notes2Skills framework converts laboratory notes into verifiable skills for AI agents while maintaining author uncertainty levels, addressing gaps in scientific AI development.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Scientific discovery workflows usually contain and rely heavily on lab notes, where researchers record observations, interpret uncertain results, and plan follow-up experiments. Such informative lab notes preserve evolving scientific reasoning and author uncertainty, rather than polished final results exhibited in publications, providing a valuable opportunity for AI to engage in scientific exploration at a more comprehensive and deeper level. However, most prior work on scientific text focuses on papers, protocols, or structured databases, leaving informal laboratory notes underexplored as inputs to AI agents for science. This gap matters because lab notes often intermingle validated observations, tentative judgments, and possible experimental next steps within the same passage. If these signals are conflated, an AI agent may mistake uncertain scientific judgments for confirmed conclusions or executable actions. To this end, we present Notes2Skills, a two-stage framework for turning lab notebooks into verifiable skills for scientific AI agents while preserving the author's certainty. Across seven conditions and three wet-lab sessions, Notes2Skills is the only configuration that neither mistakes uncertain notes for firm instructions nor discards firm ones. We show that certainty preservation is the missing piece between lab notebooks and reliable agent skills, opening a path toward safer AI co-scientist systems.
affiliations: Southern University of Science and Technology
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Сохранение уверенности: ключ к безопасным научным AI-ассистентам
ru.desc: В работе предложена система Notes2Skills, которая преобразует лабораторные записи в проверяемые навыки для AI-агентов, сохраняя уровень уверенности автора. Ключевое отличие подхода в том, что система не путает неопределённые предположения с подтверждёнными результатами, что критично для безопасной работы AI в научных исследованиях. Авторы показали, что лабораторные записи содержат смешанные сигналы — подтвержденные наблюдения, предварительные суждения и планы экспериментов, и их правильное разделение требует сохранения информации об уверенности автора. Фреймворк прошёл тестирование на семи конфигурациях и трёх реальных лабораторных сессиях, демонстрируя способность надежно преобразовывать неформальные заметки в инструкции для научных AI-систем.

en.title: Transforming Lab Notes into Reliable AI Skills
en.desc: The Notes2Skills framework transforms laboratory notes into reliable skills for AI agents while keeping track of the author's uncertainty. This approach addresses the challenge of using informal lab notes, which often contain a mix of confirmed observations and uncertain judgments. By implementing a two-stage process, Notes2Skills ensures that AI agents do not confuse uncertain notes with definitive instructions. This preservation of certainty is crucial for developing safer AI systems that can assist in scientific exploration effectively.

zh.title: 将实验室笔记转化为AI可验证技能的创新框架
zh.desc: Notes2Skills框架将实验室笔记转化为可验证的技能，供AI代理使用，同时保持作者的不确定性水平。这一方法解决了科学AI发展中的空白，因为大多数研究集中在论文和结构化数据库上，而实验室笔记却被忽视。实验室笔记包含了研究者的观察、对不确定结果的解释以及后续实验的计划，提供了AI进行科学探索的宝贵机会。通过Notes2Skills，AI代理能够更准确地理解实验室笔记中的不确定性，从而避免将不确定的科学判断误认为已确认的结论。

papers.title: Tmax: A simple recipe for terminal agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel RL training approach for terminal agents achieves superior performance using a simplified recipe and expanded dataset, enabling effective training with fewer parameters than previous methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Terminal-using agents have quickly become the most popular downstream application of language models (LMs). Despite their prevalence, relatively little academic work has examined RL-based training of these models, likely due to difficult benchmarks, a lack of data, and a lack of simple baseline recipes. We present Tmax, the strongest open RL recipe for terminal agents to date, bringing open data recipes closer to the frontier. While simple, our recipe achieves 27\% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 with only 9B parameters, outperforming much larger models from prior work. Concretely, we generate data using a novel taxonomy, combining difficulty control, personas, and verifier diversification, which allows us to cheaply generate large amounts of terminal environments for RL and SFT training. We open-source our terminal dataset, which is over 2.5x larger than previously released terminal-agent datasets. We then train open-weight models using RL with our data, using a simple, outcome-only recipe. We release our data, models, and code as a strong baseline for future open academic work on terminal agents at https://github.com/hamishivi/tmax.

ru.title: Простой рецепт обучения терминальных агентов с большими открытыми данными
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый подход Tmax для обучения терминальных агентов с использованием reinforcement learning, который демонстрирует превосходные результаты благодаря упрощённой методике и расширенному датасету. Авторы разработали инновационную таксономию для генерации данных, объединяющую контроль сложности, различные персоны и диверсификацию верификаторов, позволяющую дёшево создавать большое количество окружений для обучения. Они выпустили открытый датасет терминальных задач размером более чем в 2.5 раза больше, чем предыдущие публичные наборы данных. Модель с 9 миллиардами параметров достигает 27% точности на бенчмарке Terminal-Bench 2.0, превосходя значительно более крупные модели из предыдущих исследований.

en.title: Tmax: Simplifying RL Training for Terminal Agents
en.desc: This paper introduces Tmax, a new reinforcement learning (RL) training method specifically designed for terminal agents, which are applications of language models. The approach simplifies the training process while utilizing a larger dataset, allowing for effective model training with fewer parameters compared to previous methods. Tmax achieves a notable performance of 27% on the Terminal-Bench 2.0 benchmark using only 9 billion parameters, outperforming larger models. The authors also provide an open-source dataset that is significantly larger than existing ones, facilitating further research in this area.

zh.title: 终端代理的强化学习新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的强化学习（RL）训练方法，专门针对终端代理，能够在参数更少的情况下实现优越的性能。我们介绍的Tmax是迄今为止最强大的开放RL训练方案，利用简化的配方和扩展的数据集，使得训练过程更加高效。通过生成数据的创新分类法，我们能够以低成本生成大量终端环境用于RL和监督微调（SFT）训练。我们将数据、模型和代码开源，为未来的终端代理研究提供了强有力的基准。

papers.title: DailyReport: An Open-ended Benchmark for Evaluating Search Agents on Daily Search Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract Search agents face challenges in real-world evaluation due to limited benchmarks and coarse metrics, necessitating more nuanced assessment approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Search Agents (SAs) typically leverage large language models (LLMs) to support complex information-seeking tasks by autonomously exploring web sources and synthesizing information into comprehensive responses. For SAs evaluation, prior benchmarks mainly focus on specialized tasks that are unlikely to arise in real-world user scenarios. Moreover, their reliance on coarse task-level rubrics often limits evaluation interpretability. To bridge this gap, we introduce DailyReport, an open-ended benchmark to evaluate SA capabilities on daily search tasks. It contains 150 open-ended tasks with 3,546 associated rubrics, capturing widely discussed and timely information demands of real-world users. Each task is decomposed into subtasks and evaluated with cascade rubrics across disentangled dimensions. Through cascade performance attribution and user-centric aggregation, we derive highly interpretable scores for each dimension, along with a user preference score. Our results on 17 agentic systems show that current systems still fall short of users' expectations. To facilitate future research, our dataset and code are made publicly available at https://github.com/AGI-Eval-Official/DailyReport.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Оценка поисковых агентов через призму реальных информационных потребностей пользователей
ru.desc: В работе представлен DailyReport — новый бенчмарк для оценки поисковых агентов, которые используют большие языковые модели для автономного поиска информации в интернете. Авторы критикуют существующие методы оценки за то, что они сосредоточены на специализированных задачах и используют грубые метрики, не отражающие реальные потребности пользователей. Предложенный бенчмарк содержит 150 открытых задач с 3546 оценочными рубриками, разложенными на подзадачи и оцениваемыми по различным измерениям с помощью каскадных рубрик. Экспериментальные результаты на 17 агентных системах показывают, что текущие решения еще не соответствуют ожиданиям пользователей, а датасет и код открыты для сообщества исследователей.

en.title: Enhancing Search Agent Evaluation with DailyReport
