en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by search agents (SAs) in real-world evaluations, highlighting the inadequacy of existing benchmarks and metrics. It introduces DailyReport, a new open-ended benchmark designed to assess SA capabilities on daily search tasks, featuring 150 tasks and over 3,500 rubrics. The evaluation method employs cascade rubrics that break down tasks into subtasks, allowing for detailed performance attribution and user-centric scoring. The findings indicate that current search agents do not meet user expectations, emphasizing the need for improved evaluation frameworks in the field.

zh.title: 提升搜索代理评估的可解释性与实用性
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了搜索代理在现实世界评估中的挑战，尤其是现有基准和评估指标的局限性。我们提出了DailyReport，一个开放式基准，用于评估搜索代理在日常搜索任务中的能力。该基准包含150个开放式任务和3546个相关评估标准，旨在捕捉真实用户的信息需求。通过级联性能归因和用户中心的聚合方法，我们为每个维度提供了高度可解释的评分，并揭示了当前系统未能满足用户期望的现状。

papers.title: Exploring the Design Space of Reward Backpropagation for Flow Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract FlowBP addresses limitations in flow matching model alignment by using a surrogate trajectory framework that reduces memory usage and gradient chaining while maintaining performance across multiple text-to-image models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Aligning text-to-image flow matching models with human preferences via direct reward backpropagation is sample-efficient but hampered by two well-known pathologies: activations cannot be stored across the full sampling trajectory at modern model scale, and chained Jacobian products across steps inflate the reward gradient as it travels back to early indices. Connector-based methods, such as LeapAlign, address these issues by replacing the full backward trajectory with a short pinned path, highlighting a useful decoupling between sampling and optimization. However, the quality of the resulting gradient depends on how accurately this short path approximates the full rollout, especially over long intervals. We propose FlowBP, a unified surrogate-trajectory framework that treats the backward trajectory itself as the design object. FlowBP keeps a no-gradient cached rollout for sampling, then builds a lightweight backward surrogate from cached and selectively re-forwarded velocities. This view separates four choices: the reward-model input, active set, integration weights, and bridge coupling, and recovers prior direct-gradient methods as particular settings. Within this framework, we instantiate three variants: FlowBP-Sparse uses sparse Euler reconstruction, FlowBP-Bridge adds controlled bridge coupling, and FlowBP-Lagrange raises the order of leap quadrature. All three bound memory by the active-set size and limit gradient chaining to at most one Jacobian factor. Across SD3.5-M, FLUX.1-dev, and FLUX.2-Klein-base on preference, quality, and compositional metrics, the three variants improve over direct-gradient baselines on most metrics.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Эффективное выравнивание flux моделей через суррогатные траектории
ru.desc: FlowBP — это новый подход к выравниванию моделей flow matching для text-to-image генерации с предпочтениями человека. Метод решает две критические проблемы: невозможность хранения активаций на всей траектории генерации и экспоненциальный рост градиентов при обратном распространении через множество шагов. FlowBP использует суррогатную траекторию для обратного прохода, построенную из кэшированных скоростей и выборочно пересчитанных значений, что позволяет эффективно оптимизировать градиенты награды. Авторы предлагают три варианта метода (Sparse, Bridge и Lagrange), которые демонстрируют улучшение качества генерации при значительном снижении потребления памяти.

en.title: Optimizing Text-to-Image Models with FlowBP: Efficient Gradient Management
en.desc: FlowBP introduces a new framework to improve the alignment of text-to-image models by addressing issues related to memory usage and gradient chaining. It utilizes a surrogate trajectory approach that allows for efficient sampling without storing full activations, thus reducing computational overhead. The framework separates key design choices, enabling better control over the optimization process and improving the quality of gradients. Three variants of FlowBP demonstrate enhanced performance on various metrics compared to traditional direct-gradient methods, showcasing its effectiveness in aligning models with human preferences.

zh.title: FlowBP：优化文本到图像模型的创新框架
zh.desc: FlowBP提出了一种新的框架，用于解决流匹配模型对齐中的一些限制。它通过使用替代轨迹的方法，减少了内存使用和梯度链的影响，同时在多个文本到图像模型中保持了良好的性能。该方法将反向轨迹视为设计对象，并通过缓存的无梯度轨迹和选择性重新前向的速度构建轻量级的反向替代。FlowBP的三个变体在多个指标上超越了直接梯度基线，展示了其在样本效率和优化质量上的优势。

papers.title: Safe Few-Step Generation via Velocity Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract VESFlow is a training-free safety method for flow matching-based text-to-image generation that edits velocity fields to ensure safe output while maintaining prompt integrity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Flow matching has recently emerged as a strong paradigm for state-of-the-art text-to-image (T2I) generation, enabling high-quality generation with a small number of sampling steps. As these models are increasingly integrated into real-world applications, ensuring safe and non-sensitive content generation has become a critical requirement. However, adapting safety and concept removal methods to this new generation framework remains an open challenge. Specifically, prior methods largely rely on iterative trajectory steering across a number of denoising steps or on CLIP-centric prompt embedding manipulation. These design assumptions pose fundamental bottlenecks for safety in flow matching-based T2I generation, where limited sampling steps constrain iterative correction and modern context-aware text encoders diminish the effectiveness of embedding-level interventions. In this paper, we propose VESFlow, a training-free safety method tailored to flow matching with extremely few sampling steps. Leveraging the fact that flow matching models learn the marginal velocity, we directly edit the velocity field via a safe-conditional posterior. VESFlow steers the trajectory toward safe outputs while leaving the conditioning prompt unchanged. Building on the observation that VESFlow leaves outputs unchanged under benign prompts, we further introduce a risk score-based filtering that bypasses velocity editing to reduce computational cost while preserving benign prompt generation. Based on this filtering, we propose VESFlow+, a stronger variant of VESFlow that not only edits the velocity toward the safe direction, but also pushes it away from the unsafe direction. Experimental results show that VESFlow+ removes the target concept, reducing the attack success rate by NudeNet to 6.3% on Ring-A-Bell and 6.8% on MMA-Diffusion on the 4-step MeanFlow model, while preserving fidelity on benign prompts.

ru.title: Прямое редактирование векторных полей для безопасной генерации изображений
ru.desc: VESFlow — это метод безопасности без обучения для генерации текста в изображение на основе flow matching, который редактирует поля скоростей для обеспечения безопасного вывода. Метод использует тот факт, что модели flow matching обучаются маргинальной скорости, и напрямую редактирует векторное поле через безопасный условный апостериор. Авторы вводят фильтрацию на основе оценки риска и предлагают VESFlow+, который направляет траекторию как в сторону безопасных, так и в сторону от опасных выходов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод эффективно удаляет нежелательные концепции с минимальной потерей качества на безобидных запросах.

en.title: Safe and Efficient Text-to-Image Generation with VESFlow
en.desc: VESFlow is a novel safety method designed for text-to-image generation that does not require training. It modifies the velocity fields in flow matching models to ensure that the generated images are safe while keeping the original prompts intact. This approach addresses the challenge of ensuring non-sensitive content generation in real-world applications, where traditional methods may struggle due to limited sampling steps. Additionally, VESFlow+ enhances this method by incorporating risk score-based filtering, which optimizes computational efficiency while effectively reducing the generation of unsafe content.

zh.title: VESFlow：安全生成的创新方法
zh.desc: VESFlow是一种无需训练的安全方法，旨在确保基于流匹配的文本到图像生成的安全性，同时保持提示的完整性。该方法通过直接编辑速度场来引导生成过程，确保输出内容安全。VESFlow+是VESFlow的增强版本，不仅将速度调整到安全方向，还能有效避免不安全的生成。实验结果表明，VESFlow+在保持良好提示的同时，显著降低了攻击成功率。

papers.title: Toward Open Weight Models Without Risks: Separating Public and Private Capabilities in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Tiered Language Models (TLMs) provide a framework for releasing large language models with configurable capability levels through secret keys that modify computation graphs while maintaining public model integrity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Open-weight Large Language Models (LLMs) enable scientific progress and broad deployment. However, they make it difficult to control access to sensitive capabilities. Current practice either suppresses dangerous capabilities before release or mediates access through closed services that use specialized model variants, input/output monitors, and API permissions. The former is susceptible to jailbreaks while sacrificing capability for all users to mitigate the risks posed by a few, and the latter is fundamentally incompatible with open-weight release. In this paper, we propose Tiered Language Models (TLMs), where a single set of released weights supports multiple capability levels. In its default public configuration, a TLM behaves as a conventional LLM. A compact secret key specifies a permutation over a small parameter subset, inducing an alternative computation graph over the same weights that exposes additional capabilities. We develop a training protocol that jointly pretrains both configurations from scratch, then fine-tunes the keyed configuration on private data with regularization to preserve the public model's behavior. We pretrain 180M- and 650M-parameter TLMs and demonstrate that the keyed configuration can acquire a new language, gain instruction-following ability, and memorize private factual knowledge, whereas the public configuration exhibits none of these capabilities. Moreover, we show that our approach extends naturally to multiple hierarchical tiers. Because authorization operates on the model's weight structure rather than in the input space, the mechanism resists fine-tuning-based extraction and partial key compromise. In general, TLMs take a step toward reconciling open-weight release with selective capability control.

ru.title: Многоуровневые модели: открытые веса с контролируемым доступом
ru.desc: В статье предложена новая архитектура Tiered Language Models (TLMs), которая позволяет выпускать один набор весов модели с несколькими уровнями функциональности, управляемыми секретными ключами. Модель обучается совместно в двух конфигурациях: публичной (базовое поведение) и защищённой (расширенные возможности), включая изучение новых языков и приватных данных. Механизм авторизации работает на уровне структуры весов модели, а не входных данных, что обеспечивает устойчивость к попыткам извлечения информации через fine-tuning. Этот подход позволяет сбалансировать открытое распространение моделей с контролем доступа к чувствительным функциям.

en.title: Unlocking Capabilities with Tiered Language Models
en.desc: Tiered Language Models (TLMs) introduce a novel way to manage access to different capabilities of large language models while keeping the model weights open to the public. By using a secret key, TLMs can modify the computation graph to unlock additional functionalities without altering the original model's integrity. This approach allows for a single model to operate at various capability levels, enabling users to access advanced features securely. The training process ensures that both the public and keyed configurations are optimized, allowing the model to learn new skills while maintaining its core behavior.

zh.title: 分层语言模型：开放与控制的平衡
zh.desc: 分层语言模型（TLMs）提供了一种框架，可以通过秘密密钥调整计算图，从而释放具有可配置能力水平的大型语言模型，同时保持公共模型的完整性。TLM的默认公共配置表现得像传统的语言模型，而通过紧凑的秘密密钥可以指定小参数子集的排列，诱导出一个替代计算图，暴露出额外的能力。我们开发了一种训练协议，联合预训练这两种配置，并在私有数据上微调带密钥的配置，以保持公共模型的行为。TLMs的设计使得开放权重发布与选择性能力控制之间的矛盾得以缓解。

papers.title: PoLAR: Factorizing Extent and Mode in Latent Actions for Robot Policy Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract PoLAR introduces a geometrically structured latent action representation in hyperbolic space that separates transition extent from transition mode, improving robotic policy learning performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Latent action pretraining learns representations of visual change from pairs of observations, but existing methods typically encode each transition as a single unstructured representation that entangles transition extent and transition mode. We introduce Polar Latent Actions with Radial structure (PoLAR), which imposes a radial-direction structure on latent actions, encouraging radius to encode transition extent and direction to retain transition mode. PoLAR uses temporal offset between two observations as a weak proxy for transition extent, encouraging latent action from observation pairs separated by larger temporal gaps to occupy larger radii. We instantiate this structure in hyperbolic space, whose expanding volume with radius offers a natural fit for more diverse transition modes at larger extents. Across in-task and large-scale pretraining settings, PoLAR improves downstream policy performance in simulation and real-world robot experiments, outperforming latent action baselines and strong pretrained VLAs. These results suggest that the geometry of the latent action space is an important design choice for transferring visual pretraining to downstream robot policy learning.

ru.title: Геометрия скрытого пространства действий: разделение масштаба и типа переходов в гиперболическом пространстве
ru.desc: PoLAR представляет новый подход к предварительному обучению действий робота, используя гиперболическое пространство для структурированного представления скрытых действий. Метод разделяет два аспекта переходов между наблюдениями: масштаб изменения (кодируется радиусом) и тип изменения (кодируется направлением). Авторы используют временной промежуток между парами наблюдений как слабый сигнал для обучения радиальной структуры, так как большие временные разрывы соответствуют более значительным переходам. Экспериментальные результаты на симуляторах и реальных роботах показывают, что геометрическая структура скрытого пространства действий критически важна для эффективного переноса визуального предобучения на задачи управления роботом.

en.title: Revolutionizing Robotic Learning with Geometric Action Representation
en.desc: The paper introduces PoLAR, a new method for representing actions in robotic policy learning using a geometric structure in hyperbolic space. This approach separates the extent of a transition from its mode, allowing for more effective learning of robotic behaviors. By using the temporal gap between observations as a guide, PoLAR encourages a radial structure where the radius indicates how far a transition goes, while the direction indicates its type. The results show that this method significantly enhances performance in both simulated and real-world robotic tasks compared to existing techniques.

zh.title: PoLAR：提升机器人策略学习的潜在动作表示
zh.desc: PoLAR提出了一种在双曲空间中具有几何结构的潜在动作表示，能够将转移幅度与转移模式分开，从而提高机器人策略学习的性能。通过观察对的视觉变化进行潜在动作预训练，PoLAR采用径向结构，鼓励半径编码转移幅度，而方向则保留转移模式。该方法利用两个观察之间的时间偏移作为转移幅度的弱代理，促使时间间隔较大的观察对的潜在动作占据更大的半径。实验结果表明，PoLAR在模拟和真实机器人实验中均优于传统的潜在动作基线，表明潜在动作空间的几何结构对视觉预训练向下游机器人策略学习的转移至关重要。

papers.title: Connect the Dots: Training LLMs for Long-Lifecycle Agents with Cross-Domain Generalization Via Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models can be trained through reinforcement learning to develop a meta-capability enabling continuous learning and adaptation across long sequences of tasks in dynamic environments.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct This work presents a general framework for training large language models (LLMs) to "Connect the Dots" (CoD), a meta-capability required by long-lifecycle agents: as an LLM-based AI agent gets deployed in an environment, it solves a long sequence of tasks while continuously exploring the environment, learning from its own experiences, and iteratively self-updating its context about the environment, thereby achieving progressively better performance on future tasks conditioned on the updated context. Major components of the CoD framework include: (1) algorithm design and infrastructure for end-to-end reinforcement learning (RL) with long rollout sequences interleaving solve-task and update-context episodes; (2) tasks and environments for incentivizing and eliciting the targeted meta-capability in LLMs during training, as well as for faithfully measuring progress during evaluation. We present proof-of-concept implementations of the CoD framework, including a GRPO-style RL algorithm with fine-grained credit assignment, as well as tasks and environments tailored to the targeted meta-capability (rather than domain-specific LLM capabilities or standard task-by-task RL). Empirical results validate the efficacy of end-to-end RL training in the CoD setting, and demonstrate the potential for out-of-distribution generalization -- within the training domains, across different domains, and from CoD to Ralph-loop settings -- of the elicited meta-capability. Our investigation of CoD connects several lines of prior works, and opens up new opportunities for advancing LLMs and AI agents. To facilitate further research and applications, we release our implementations at https://github.com/agentscope-ai/Trinity-RFT/tree/research/cod/examples/research_cod.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Агенты, которые учатся на своих ошибках: мета-обучение для адаптивных систем
ru.desc: В этой работе представлена система обучения больших языковых моделей через reinforcement learning для развития мета-способности к непрерывному обучению и адаптации.框架Connect the Dots позволяет LLM-агентам накапливать знания о среде через длинные последовательности задач, обновляя свой контекст на основе собственного опыта. Авторы разработали алгоритм GRPO-стиля с детальным распределением кредита и специализированные среды для обучения именно этой мета-способности. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что такое обучение обобщается за пределы распределения обучающих данных и работает как внутри одного домена, так и при переносе на новые задачи.

en.title: Empowering LLMs with Continuous Learning through 'Connect the Dots'
en.desc: This paper introduces a framework for training large language models (LLMs) to enhance their ability to learn continuously and adapt to new tasks in changing environments. The framework, called 'Connect the Dots' (CoD), allows LLMs to solve a series of tasks while learning from their experiences and updating their understanding of the environment. Key components include a reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm designed for long sequences of tasks and specific environments that encourage the development of this meta-capability. The results show that this approach improves the models' performance and their ability to generalize across different tasks and domains.

zh.title: 连接点：大型语言模型的持续学习能力
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种训练大型语言模型（LLMs）的方法，旨在通过强化学习实现持续学习和适应能力。该框架名为“连接点”（CoD），使得AI代理能够在动态环境中解决一系列任务，同时不断探索和更新其环境上下文。CoD框架的主要组成部分包括端到端强化学习算法设计和任务环境，以激励和评估LLMs的目标能力。实验证明了在CoD设置下的端到端强化学习训练的有效性，并展示了在不同领域和任务之间的泛化潜力。

papers.title: Causal Discovery in the Era of Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Language models should assist causal discovery workflows by providing contextual support and explanations rather than generating causal conclusions, as demonstrated through a platform that integrates data analysis and expert knowledge.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent attempts to combine large language models (LLMs) with causal discovery ask models to infer pairwise directions, propose graph structures, or inject language-model outputs as priors and constraints. These approaches promise faster analysis, but they also obscure whether a causal evidence is supported by data and assumptions or by textual associations, prompt artifacts and hallucinated mechanisms. We argue for a different role for agents in causal discovery. Agents should inspect data, retrieve context, explain method assumptions and clarify graph outputs, but they should not supply edges, orientations, priors, constraints or causal conclusions. We propose the principle that agents assist the workflow, while causal claims remain grounded in data, explicit assumptions, formal algorithms, diagnostics and user or domain-expert decisions. We instantiate this principle in causal-learn+, an online platform that coordinates data analysis, preprocessing, method recommendation, expert-knowledge incorporation, formal discovery and interpretation around the algorithmic ecosystem of causal-learn. A case study on Big Five personality data illustrates agent-assisted pipeline of causal discovery without turning language-model unreliability into causal evidence. The platform is available at causallearn.com.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Языковые модели как помощники, а не судьи причинности
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый подход к использованию языковых моделей в задачах обнаружения причинно-следственных связей. Авторы утверждают, что LLM должны играть вспомогательную роль — объяснять предположения алгоритмов, предоставлять контекст и интерпретировать результаты, но не генерировать сами причинные связи и ориентации графов. Они разработали платформу causal-learn+, которая интегрирует анализ данных, рекомендации методов, знания экспертов и формальные алгоритмы обнаружения причинности. На примере анализа данных о личностных чертах демонстрируется, как можно использовать агентов для поддержки рабочего процесса, сохраняя при этом надёжность причинных выводов на основе данных и явных предположений.

en.title: Empowering Causal Discovery with Language Model Support
en.desc: This paper discusses the role of language models in causal discovery, emphasizing that they should support the process rather than make causal conclusions. It highlights the risks of relying on language models for inferring causal relationships, as they may produce misleading results based on textual associations rather than solid data. The authors propose a framework where language models assist by providing context, explaining assumptions, and clarifying outputs, while leaving causal claims to be determined by data and expert judgment. The paper introduces a platform called causal-learn+ that integrates these principles into a structured workflow for causal analysis.

zh.title: 智能体助力因果发现，数据为王
zh.desc: 本文探讨了语言模型在因果发现工作流程中的作用，强调它们应提供上下文支持和解释，而不是直接生成因果结论。研究表明，现有方法虽然能加快分析速度，但可能会混淆因果证据的来源。我们主张，智能体应专注于数据检查、上下文检索和方法假设解释，而不应提供因果边缘或结论。我们提出的因果学习平台（causal-learn+）展示了如何在保持因果声明基于数据和假设的基础上，辅助因果发现的工作流程。

papers.title: Tapered Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Tapered language models allocate more parameters to earlier layers and fewer to later layers, improving performance without increasing total parameters or compute costs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Modern language models, including transformer, recurrent, and memory-based variants, share a common chassis: a stack of identical layers in which parameters are allocated uniformly across depth. This is a default inherited from the original transformer and largely unchanged since, yet a growing body of evidence suggests that layers contribute non-uniformly to the final output, with later layers refining the residual stream rather than transforming it. We ask whether parameter capacity should reflect this asymmetry. Our controlled experiment shows that, under a fixed budget, allocating more capacity to earlier layers and less to later layers improves perplexity over a uniform-width baseline, while the reverse allocation hurts. Building on this result, we introduce Tapered Language Models (TLMs), an architectural principle in which a parameter-bearing component is monotonically tapered across depth under a fixed total budget. MLPs are the natural site for this instantiation: they dominate parameter count across all modern LM families and expose width as a single, clean axis of variation. Across three model scales and four architectures (Transformer, Gated Attention, Hope-attention, and Titans), tapering MLP width via a smooth cosine schedule consistently improves perplexity and downstream benchmark performance over uniform baselines, at no additional parameter or compute cost. These findings establish depth-aware capacity allocation as a simple, architecture-agnostic axis of language model design, a free lever hidden in plain sight.

ru.title: Сужающиеся языковые модели: больше параметров в начале, меньше в конце
ru.desc: В статье исследуется новый принцип проектирования языковых моделей, при котором параметры распределяются неравномерно по глубине архитектуры. Авторы показали, что выделение большей ёмкости ранним слоям и меньшей ёмкости поздним слоям улучшает качество модели без увеличения общего числа параметров. Они предложили Tapered Language Models (TLMs), где ширина многослойных перцептронов плавно уменьшается через слои по косинусному расписанию. Эксперименты на четырёх архитектурах подтверждают, что такой подход снижает перплексию и улучшает производительность на бенчмарках без дополнительных вычислительных затрат.

en.title: Optimize Layer Capacity for Better Language Models
en.desc: This paper introduces Tapered Language Models (TLMs), which allocate more parameters to the earlier layers of a neural network and fewer to the later layers. The authors argue that this approach improves model performance without increasing the total number of parameters or computational costs. Their experiments show that this non-uniform allocation of parameters leads to better perplexity scores compared to traditional models with uniform layer widths. The findings suggest that adjusting parameter distribution based on layer contribution can enhance the effectiveness of various language model architectures.

zh.title: 优化参数分配，提升语言模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的语言模型架构，称为锥形语言模型（Tapered Language Models, TLMs），它在模型的早期层分配更多的参数，而在后期层分配更少的参数。这种方法在不增加总参数量或计算成本的情况下，显著提高了模型的性能。实验结果表明，早期层的参数容量增加可以有效降低困惑度，而后期层的参数减少则会导致性能下降。通过在多种模型规模和架构中应用这一原则，研究表明深度感知的参数分配是语言模型设计中的一个简单而有效的策略。

papers.title: Counsel: A Meta-Evaluation Dataset for Agentic Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale dataset of human-metaevaluations of LLM critiques for agentic tasks is introduced to improve the calibration and reliability of automated evaluation methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As agentic systems tackle increasingly complex multi-step tasks, evaluating their trajectories presents a major bottleneck - human annotation of a single trajectory on popular agentic benchmarks can take hours, making it difficult to scale evaluations for measuring performance or curating training data. This has driven widespread reliance on automated approaches such as LLM-as-a-judge (LLMJ) to critique agents at the process and outcome-levels at scale, however, the soundness of LLMJ critiques often goes unmeasured. Here, we introduce Counsel, the first public dataset of meta-evaluations for agentic tasks. Counsel consists of process-level critiques from open-weight LLMJs on two agent benchmarks: tau-bench (customer support agents) and DA-Code (coding agents), and human meta-evaluations of these critiques. Human annotators label critiques on each flagged error as "spot on", "correct location but poor reasoning", or "should not have flagged", achieving reliable inter-annotator agreement (Krippendorff's alpha of 0.78). The resulting dataset stratifies LLMJ critiques by human alignment across both error location within a trajectory and reasoning quality, serving as valuable data to calibrate, improve, or train LLMJs for agents. Comparing open-weight judges, we find that more capable judge models and more reasoning effort both enabled improved human agreement, with the strongest judge reaching ~88% agreement on location and ~65% on reasoning. Counsel is generated using open-weight models and is permissively licensed for broad community use, which we hope will enable rigorous study and improved alignment of LLM-based evaluators for agentic systems.

ru.title: Counsel: Датасет для калибровки судей среди LLM в агентных системах
ru.desc: В работе представлен Counsel — первый публичный датасет мета-оценок для агентных систем, содержащий критики от открытых моделей-судей и человеческие оценки качества этих критик. Датасет включает процессные оценки ошибок двух типов: в системах поддержки клиентов и в кодировании, где люди разметили критики по трем категориям с высокой согласованностью между аннотаторами (альфа Крипендорфа 0.78). Исследование показало, что более мощные модели-судьи и дополнительные вычисления для рассуждений улучшают согласованность с человеческими оценками, достигая ~88% на определении расположения ошибок и ~65% на качество рассуждений. Открытый датасет позволит калибровать и улучшать LLM-судей для агентных систем в будущих исследованиях.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Critiques for Better Agent Evaluation
en.desc: This paper introduces a new dataset called Counsel, which contains human evaluations of critiques made by large language models (LLMs) on agentic tasks. The dataset aims to enhance the reliability of automated evaluation methods by providing insights into the quality of LLM critiques, which are essential for assessing the performance of agentic systems. Human annotators evaluated these critiques, achieving a high level of agreement, indicating that the dataset can effectively measure the accuracy of LLM critiques. By analyzing the critiques, the dataset helps improve the calibration and training of LLMs, making them more aligned with human judgment in evaluating complex tasks.

zh.title: 提升自动评估的可靠性与校准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个大型数据集，名为Counsel，旨在提高自动评估方法的校准和可靠性。该数据集包含了对代理任务的元评估，主要由开放权重的LLM模型生成，涵盖了客户支持和编码代理的评估。人类标注者对每个标记的错误进行了分类，确保了标注的一致性。通过分析不同评估模型的表现，研究发现更强的评估模型和更深入的推理能够提高人类标注者之间的协议。

papers.title: Dense Reward for Multi-View 3D Reasoning with Global Maps and Local Views
papers.abstract: Abstract DR-MV3D presents a map-grounded learning framework with dense rewards to improve multi-view 3D visual question answering through global map construction, view-trajectory planning, and egocentric grounding.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multi-view 3D Visual Question Answering (MV3D-VQA) requires integrating partial observations into a coherent 3D scene representation and selecting informative viewpoints for multi-step spatial reasoning. However, current multimodal LLMs are typically trained with sparse, answer-level supervision, which often yields inconsistent cross-view reasoning and brittle view selection. We present DR-MV3D (Dense Reward for MV3D-VQA), a map-grounded learning framework that provides dense, verifiable rewards to supervise the reasoning process. Our approach decomposes MV3D-VQA into (i) allocentric global map construction, (ii) question-conditioned view-trajectory planning, and (iii) egocentric grounding for answer prediction. To make intermediate steps learnable without manual annotations, we introduce two rewards: a global consistency reward that aligns the predicted map with geometry-consistent pseudo targets from frozen 3D vision foundation models (e.g., VGGT + SAM3), and a local trajectory reward that supervises ordered viewpoint selection. We optimize the full pipeline with trajectory-level policy optimization (GRPO). Experiments on MindCube, VSI-Bench, and BLINK (MV) show that DR-MV3D consistently improves over strong multi-image baselines, supporting the effectiveness of process-level dense supervision for multi-view 3D reasoning.
affiliations: Graduate School of Artificial Intelligence, KAIST, Republic of Korea

ru.title: Плотные награды для многовидового 3D рассуждения через построение карт и оптимизацию траекторий
ru.desc: DR-MV3D представляет фреймворк обучения с плотными наградами для улучшения ответов на вопросы по мультивидовым 3D сценам. Подход декомпозирует задачу на три этапа: построение глобальной карты в аллоцентрической системе координат, планирование траектории обзора обусловленное вопросом и эгоцентрическое обоснование предсказания ответа. Для обучения промежуточных шагов используются две функции награды: глобальная консистентность, выравнивающая предсказанную карту с псевдо-целями от замороженных моделей 3D vision, и локальная награда траектории для контроля выбора точек обзора. Оптимизация всего конвейера проводится методом policy optimization на уровне траекторий, достигая улучшения над базовыми подходами на нескольких бенчмарках.

en.title: Enhancing 3D Visual Question Answering with Dense Rewards
en.desc: The paper introduces DR-MV3D, a framework designed to enhance multi-view 3D visual question answering (MV3D-VQA) by utilizing dense rewards for better reasoning. It focuses on constructing a global map, planning view trajectories, and grounding answers in an egocentric manner. Unlike traditional methods that rely on sparse supervision, DR-MV3D employs a global consistency reward and a local trajectory reward to guide the learning process. The results demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms existing baselines in multi-image scenarios, highlighting the benefits of dense supervision in complex 3D reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 基于地图的密集奖励提升多视角3D问答
zh.desc: DR-MV3D提出了一种基于地图的学习框架，通过密集奖励来改善多视角3D视觉问答。该框架包括全局地图构建、视角轨迹规划和自我中心化的基础，旨在将部分观察整合为一致的3D场景表示。我们引入了全局一致性奖励和局部轨迹奖励，以监督推理过程并优化视角选择。实验结果表明，DR-MV3D在多个基准测试中表现优异，验证了过程级密集监督在多视角3D推理中的有效性。

papers.title: PolicyTrim: Boosting Intrinsic Policy Efficiency of Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: Abstract PolicyTrim is a reinforcement learning-based framework that enhances VLA model efficiency by extending reliable action chunk lengths and reducing redundant physical steps through dynamic exploration and redundancy-aware rewards.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models provide a unified paradigm for robotic manipulation, yet their real-world deployment is often bottlenecked by execution efficiency. While existing efforts predominantly focus on compute-centric efficiency to reduce per-step inference latency, the intrinsic policy efficiency of these models remains largely unexplored. Policy efficiency is fundamentally affected by two factors, namely the effective executable length of predicted action chunks and the total physical steps required to complete a task. These two factors jointly determine the total number of forward inference calls during execution. We observe that current VLA policies struggle with planning unreliability and action redundancy, suffering from severe prediction degradation at the tail of action chunks and tending to generate unnecessarily redundant physical steps. To address this, we propose PolicyTrim, a reinforcement learning-based post-training framework that extends the reliable action chunk length and reduces redundant physical steps. For reliable chunk extension, we employ a dynamic exploration strategy that explicitly rewards the successful completion of longer executable lengths, progressively pushing the trustworthy prediction horizon to its empirical limit. For step efficiency, we design a redundancy-aware reward that directly favors successful task completions with fewer steps while penalizing unreproducible shortcuts, effectively eliminating redundant physical actions. Extensive experiments across three benchmarks and three VLA models demonstrate that PolicyTrim improves action chunk utilization by 3times and reduces physical execution steps by 51.4\%. Ultimately, our framework delivers up to a 5.83times end-to-end deployment speedup without compromising task success rates.

ru.title: Обрезка политики: RL-оптимизация VLA для эффективного управления роботом
ru.desc: PolicyTrim — это фреймворк на основе обучения с подкреплением, который повышает эффективность Vision-Language-Action моделей путём расширения длины надёжных последовательностей действий и снижения избыточных физических шагов. Метод использует динамическую стратегию исследования, которая награждает успешное выполнение более длинных предсказываемых последовательностей, постепенно расширяя горизонт предсказания. Авторы вводят функцию награды, учитывающую избыточность действий, которая поощряет завершение задач с минимальным числом шагов и штрафует невоспроизводимые «ярлыки». Экспериментальные результаты показывают трёхкратное улучшение использования последовательностей действий и 51,4% сокращение физических шагов, что даёт ускорение развёртывания в 5,83 раза.

en.title: Boosting Robotic Efficiency with PolicyTrim
en.desc: PolicyTrim is a novel framework that enhances the efficiency of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in robotic manipulation by optimizing action execution. It utilizes reinforcement learning to extend the length of reliable action chunks and minimize unnecessary physical steps, addressing issues of planning unreliability and action redundancy. The framework introduces a dynamic exploration strategy that rewards longer, successful action sequences and a redundancy-aware reward system that encourages fewer physical actions for task completion. Experimental results show significant improvements in action chunk utilization and a substantial reduction in execution steps, leading to faster deployment without sacrificing task success rates.

zh.title: 提升VLA模型效率的PolicyTrim框架
zh.desc: PolicyTrim是一个基于强化学习的框架，旨在提高VLA模型的效率。它通过动态探索和关注冗余奖励，延长可靠的动作块长度并减少冗余的物理步骤。该框架解决了当前VLA策略在规划不可靠性和动作冗余方面的问题，从而提高了执行效率。实验结果表明，PolicyTrim能够将动作块利用率提高3倍，并将物理执行步骤减少51.4%。

papers.title: Vera: A Layered Diffusion Model for Content-Preserving Video Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract Vera is a layered diffusion framework that preserves video content during editing by generating edit layers and alpha mattes through a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video diffusion models have enabled remarkable progress in video generation and editing. However, content preservation remains a core challenge: existing methods regenerate every pixel and often alter elements that should remain unchanged, such as characters or background scenes. We introduce Vera, a layered diffusion framework for content-preserving video editing. Instead of regenerating the entire video, Vera generates an edit layer along with an alpha matte for compositing with the source video, separating creative editing from content preservation by design. To encourage coherent composition with the source video, we extend the text-to-video DiT into a Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture, with separate DiTs for each layer that interact through joint self-attention. To support the training of Vera, we further construct a high-quality layered dataset with accurate alpha mattes, diverse scenes and dynamics, and visual effects. Across our quantitative benchmark and human preference study, Vera outperforms leading open-source video editing models in content preservation while remaining competitive in edit quality, using 486K frames of layered training data.

ru.title: Послойное редактирование видео с сохранением оригинального контента
ru.desc: Vera — это фреймворк на основе диффузионных моделей для редактирования видео, который сохраняет исходный контент путём генерации слоёв редактирования и масок прозрачности. Вместо переделки всех пикселей видео, модель генерирует отдельный слой с альфа-маттом, который затем композируется с исходным видео. Архитектура Mixture-of-Transformers использует несколько трансформеров DiT, специализированных на разных слоях, которые взаимодействуют через совместное само-внимание. Авторы создали высококачественный датасет с послойной разметкой и показали, что Vera превосходит существующие методы в сохранении контента при сохранении качества редактирования.

en.title: Vera: Preserving Video Content While Editing
en.desc: Vera is a new framework designed for video editing that focuses on keeping the original content intact while making changes. It uses a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture to create separate layers for edits and alpha mattes, which helps in combining new edits with the original video without altering important elements. This approach allows for creative editing while ensuring that characters and backgrounds remain unchanged. Vera has been trained on a large dataset with high-quality layers, showing better performance in preserving content compared to other video editing models.

zh.title: Vera：视频编辑中的内容保护新方法
zh.desc: Vera是一种分层扩散框架，旨在视频编辑中保持内容不变。它通过Mixture-of-Transformers架构生成编辑层和alpha matte，从而实现创意编辑与内容保护的分离。与传统方法不同，Vera不再重新生成整个视频，而是生成一个编辑层与源视频进行合成。通过构建高质量的分层数据集，Vera在内容保护方面优于现有的开源视频编辑模型，同时在编辑质量上也保持竞争力。

papers.title: A Verifiable Search Is Not a Learnable Chain-of-Thought
papers.abstract: Abstract Training models on chain-of-thought demonstrations fails for tasks requiring backtracking search because the forward derivation cannot be faithfully imitated, demonstrating a fundamental limitation in learning search procedures through demonstration.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct It is tempting to assume any task solvable by a short program can be taught to a model as its chain-of-thought: write the steps out, fine-tune, and the model follows. This paper shows the assumption fails for an identifiable class of procedures. The testbed is nine reasoning tasks, each from a deterministic generator; public and hidden splits share generators, so held-out data proxies test accuracy. I reverse-engineer the generators into Python solvers, render them as chain-of-thought, and distill into a rank-<= 32 LoRA over a 30B (3.5B-active) Nemotron model. Forward-computable tasks install readily: lookup/arithmetic and an 8-bit boolean task transfer (>= 0.99 and 0.68). Cryptarithm does not: distilling its backtracking search holds at 0.01-0.07 across eleven chain-of-thought designs, RL from verifiable rewards, and self-training, even though a search solver answers 71% of instances. This is not a capability gap. The model does the arithmetic on 97-100% of lines and ranks the correct cipher in its top eight on 71%; it cannot carry the search forward as a left-to-right derivation. Fine-tuning learns the shape of a verifiable elimination step while its verdicts become unconditional templates, correct only 16-57% of the time ("verdict-as-token"). The ceiling holds across backbones from 3B to 671B and across fine-tuning and prompting; a controlled intervention isolates the cause: revealing the cipher key, which turns the derivation forward, lifts the same instances from 0.03 to 0.57. When a procedure's only solution is search over information-free structure, no faithful forward chain-of-thought exists to imitate. The task becomes learnable only by removing the search, precomputing its combinatorial core into a catalog and reducing the trace to recall plus verification; the 1st-place solution reaches Private LB 0.92 this way. What distills is memorization and verification, not search.

ru.title: Поиск с возвратом нельзя выучить по примерам — только по памяти и проверке
ru.desc: В работе показано, что обучение моделей на примерах цепочки рассуждений (chain-of-thought) неэффективно для задач, требующих поиска с возвратом, потому что нейросеть не может имитировать прямую последовательность вывода. Авторы экспериментируют с девятью задачами рассуждения, обучая модель Nemotron размером 30B через дистилляцию в LoRA ранга ≤32, и обнаруживают, что простые задачи (арифметика, поиск в таблице) усваиваются хорошо, а криптарифметика остаётся неразрешимой. Модель корректно выполняет арифметические операции и ранжирует правильное решение в топ-8, но не может провести поиск слева направо как линейную цепочку вывода, что является фундаментальным ограничением архитектуры. Решение найдено путём переформулирования задачи: вместо поиска в прямом направлении авторы предварительно вычисляют комбинаторное ядро в каталог и сводят задачу к припоминанию и проверке, достигая точности 0.92.

en.title: Learning Limits: When Chain-of-Thought Fails in Backtracking Tasks
en.desc: This paper explores the limitations of training machine learning models using chain-of-thought demonstrations for tasks that require backtracking search. It shows that while some tasks can be learned through straightforward derivation, others, like cryptarithm problems, cannot be effectively imitated by the model. The authors demonstrate that the model can perform arithmetic correctly but struggles with search-based tasks, indicating that the learning process fails when it relies solely on forward computation. Ultimately, the study suggests that for certain procedures, the only way to learn is by transforming the search into a precomputed catalog, rather than attempting to imitate the search process directly.

zh.title: 链式思维的局限性：回溯搜索的挑战
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了在链式思维演示上训练模型的局限性，特别是在需要回溯搜索的任务中。研究表明，简单的程序任务并不能通过链式思维有效地教给模型，因为模型无法忠实地模仿前向推导。通过对九个推理任务的测试，发现模型在处理前向可计算任务时表现良好，但在处理需要回溯搜索的任务时效果不佳。最终，论文指出，只有通过去除搜索过程并将其核心预计算为目录，才能使这些任务变得可学习。

papers.title: Manifold Bandits: Bayesian Curriculum Learning over the Latent Geometry of Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning approaches for improving LLM reasoning capabilities are enhanced by a Bayesian Manifold Curriculum framework that structures problem sampling based on task manifold relationships and endogenous non-stationarity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reinforcement learning (RL) is a central approach for improving reasoning capabilities in large language models (LLMs), where training efficiency depends critically on how problems are sampled during optimization. Existing adaptive curriculum learning methods typically prioritize prompts of intermediate difficulty, treating problem selection as a standard bandit problem with independent arms and overlooking the structured, heterogeneous nature of the task space. In this work, we frame problem sampling as a manifold-structured bandit problem with endogenous non-stationarity: problems are related through the model's latent representation space, and sampling decisions can steer how learning signals evolve across that space. To operationalize this perspective, we introduce Bayesian Manifold Curriculum (BMC), a structure-aware framework that organizes problems into a hierarchical task tree and applies Bayesian learning to guide sampling. Empirically, we find that different sampling strategies induce non-trivial tradeoffs between productivity (learning signal), diversity (coverage of the task manifold), and utility (evaluation relevance). These results show that prioritizing difficulty alone is insufficient for strong downstream performance, highlighting the importance of incorporating structure and type-awareness into problem sampling.

ru.title: Структурированная выборка задач через многообразие для эффективного обучения LLM
ru.desc: В статье предлагается Bayesian Manifold Curriculum (BMC) — фреймворк для улучшения способности больших языковых моделей к рассуждению через обучение с подкреплением. Авторы переосмысляют задачу выбора примеров для обучения как структурированную задачу многорукого бандита с эндогенной нестационарностью, учитывая скрытые связи между задачами в пространстве представлений модели. Фреймворк организует задачи в иерархическое дерево и использует байесовское обучение для направления стратегии выборки примеров. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что эффективная выборка требует баланса между производительностью обучения, разнообразием охватываемых задач и релевантностью оценки, что выходит за рамки простого приоритизирования по сложности.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Reasoning with Structured Problem Sampling
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to improve the reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs) using a method called Bayesian Manifold Curriculum (BMC). BMC organizes problems into a hierarchical structure based on their relationships in a latent representation space, allowing for more effective problem sampling during training. The authors argue that traditional methods, which focus solely on the difficulty of tasks, fail to capture the complex nature of the task space. By incorporating structure and type-awareness into the sampling process, BMC enhances learning efficiency and overall model performance.

zh.title: 优化问题采样，提升推理能力
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于贝叶斯流形课程的强化学习方法，以提高大型语言模型（LLM）的推理能力。该方法通过将问题采样视为流形结构的赌博问题，考虑了任务之间的关系和内生非平稳性。研究表明，单纯优先考虑问题的难度不足以提升模型性能，必须将结构和类型意识纳入问题采样中。通过引入贝叶斯流形课程框架，本文有效地组织了问题并指导了采样过程，从而实现了更高效的学习。

papers.title: Comparing Linear Probes with Mahalanobis Cosine Similarity
papers.abstract: Abstract The Mahalanobis cosine similarity provides a theoretically grounded method for comparing linear probes that correlates strongly with out-of-distribution performance metrics.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Linear probes are widely used in interpretability research and often compared by cosine similarity. The Mahalanobis cosine similarity (MCS) between two directions, which reweights the inner product by test data covariance, is a natural task-aware refinement. Ying et al. (2026) report that a probe's MCS to a reference probe trained on the out-of-distribution (OOD) data near-perfectly linearly predicts the probe's OOD AUROC (R^2 = 0.98). Here, we extend this empirical finding across models, layers, and concept domains, and prove this general phenomenon in closed form: For balanced classes whose projections are Gaussian, OOD AUROC and MCS to the reference probe are linear because both are sigmoid-shaped functions of the probe's signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) on the test data. The theory also predicts when this linearity fails, which we verify empirically. MCS offers a theoretically grounded and empirically effective alternative to Euclidean cosine similarity for comparing linear probes.

ru.title: Махаланобисово сходство: теоретически обоснованное сравнение линейных зондов
ru.desc: Статья представляет метод Махаланобисова косинусного сходства для сравнения линейных зондов, который учитывает ковариацию тестовых данных. Авторы показывают, что это сходство имеет сильную линейную связь с производительностью моделей на распределениях, отличных от обучающих. Теоретически доказано, что для сбалансированных классов с гауссовыми проекциями метрика AUROC и Махаланобисово сходство являются сигмоидными функциями отношения сигнала к шуму. Этот подход предлагает более надёжную альтернативу стандартному евклидову косинусному сходству при сравнении линейных зондов.

en.title: Mahalanobis Cosine Similarity: A Better Way to Compare Linear Probes
en.desc: The paper introduces the Mahalanobis cosine similarity (MCS) as a refined method for comparing linear probes in machine learning, particularly in interpretability research. MCS adjusts the cosine similarity by considering the covariance of test data, making it more relevant for out-of-distribution (OOD) performance metrics. The authors demonstrate that the MCS of a probe to a reference probe trained on OOD data can predict the probe's OOD AUROC with high accuracy. Additionally, they provide theoretical insights into when this linear relationship holds and when it does not, establishing MCS as a superior alternative to traditional Euclidean cosine similarity.

zh.title: 马哈拉诺比斯余弦相似度：线性探针比较的新方法
zh.desc: 马哈拉诺比斯余弦相似度（MCS）是一种理论基础扎实的方法，用于比较线性探针，并且与分布外性能指标有很强的相关性。MCS通过测试数据的协方差重新加权内积，是一种自然的任务感知改进。研究表明，线性探针的MCS与参考探针的分布外AUROC之间存在近乎完美的线性预测关系。该理论还预测了线性关系失效的情况，并通过实验证实了这一点。

papers.title: FastMix: Fast Data Mixture Optimization via Gradient Descent
papers.abstract: Abstract FASTMIX automates optimal data mixture discovery during training by formulating mixture selection as a bilevel optimization problem that jointly optimizes mixture coefficients and model parameters through iterative updates.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct While large and diverse datasets have driven recent advances in large models, identifying the optimal data mixture for pre-training and post-training remains a significant open problem. We address this challenge with FASTMIX, a novel framework that automates data mixture discovery while training only a single proxy model. Instead of relying on predefined heuristics or resource-intensive simulations, FASTMIX jointly optimizes mixture coefficients and model parameters, substantially improving efficiency and scalability over prior approaches. At the core of FASTMIX is a reformulation of mixture selection as a bilevel optimization problem. Under this reformulation, we show that optimizing mixture ratios is mathematically equivalent to assigning per-source loss weights under uniform source sampling. This embeds the mixture coefficients directly into the differentiable iterative optimization objective, enabling efficient, gradient-based optimization of both mixture and model. To solve the optimization problem, FASTMIX implements an approximate iterative optimization procedure, alternating between (i) updating model parameters on data sampled according to current mixture ratios (inner loop) and (ii) updating mixture ratios based on validation feedback (outer loop). Across pre- and post-training, FASTMIX outperforms baselines while drastically reducing search cost. Code (https://github.com/hrtan/fastmix)

ru.title: Автоматический поиск оптимальной смеси данных через двухуровневую оптимизацию
ru.desc: FASTMIX — это новый метод автоматического поиска оптимальной смеси данных во время обучения модели. Авторы формулируют задачу выбора смеси как двухуровневую оптимизацию, которая совместно оптимизирует коэффициенты смеси и параметры модели через итеративные обновления. Ключевое наблюдение состоит в том, что оптимизация коэффициентов смеси математически эквивалентна назначению весов потерь для каждого источника данных при равномерной выборке. Метод работает путём чередования обновления параметров модели на внутреннем цикле и обновления коэффициентов смеси на основе валидационной обратной связи на внешнем цикле.

en.title: Automating Optimal Data Mixture Discovery in Training
en.desc: FASTMIX is a framework that automates the discovery of optimal data mixtures during the training of machine learning models. It formulates the mixture selection process as a bilevel optimization problem, allowing for simultaneous optimization of mixture coefficients and model parameters. By using an iterative approach, FASTMIX updates model parameters based on current mixture ratios and adjusts the mixture ratios based on validation feedback. This method significantly enhances efficiency and scalability compared to traditional approaches, leading to improved performance in both pre-training and post-training phases.

zh.title: FASTMIX：自动化数据混合优化的创新框架
zh.desc: FASTMIX 是一个自动化的数据混合发现框架，通过将混合选择问题转化为双层优化问题，来优化混合系数和模型参数。该方法在训练单个代理模型的过程中，显著提高了效率和可扩展性。FASTMIX 通过迭代更新，优化混合比例和模型参数，避免了依赖预定义启发式或资源密集型模拟的缺陷。最终，FASTMIX 在预训练和后训练阶段的表现优于基线，同时大幅降低了搜索成本。

papers.title: UniverSat: Resolution- and Modality-Agnostic Transformers for Earth Observation
papers.abstract: Abstract UniverSat introduces a Universal Patch Encoder for Vision Transformers that enables robust, sensor-agnostic spatial feature extraction across diverse Earth Observation data types.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision Transformers (ViT) dominate computer vision. However, their reliance on rigid patch projectors hinders transfer to Earth Observation (EO), where input modalities, scales, and resolutions vary widely. We introduce UniverSat, a ViT-style backbone built around a Universal Patch Encoder that maps patches from arbitrary spatial, spectral, and temporal resolutions, and from both optical and non-optical sensors, into a shared embedding space with a shared set of weights. This enables training a single model on heterogeneous multimodal corpora via self-supervision, yielding robust, sensor-agnostic spatial features. We validate this approach with strong results across classification and segmentation on standard EO benchmarks from GeoBench, PANGEABench, and SpectralEarth. Our code and models are available at https://github.com/gastruc/UniverSat.
affiliations: LASTIG, Univ Gustave Eiffel, IGN, ENSG
LIGM, Ecole Nationale des Ponts et Chaussées, IP Paris, Univ Gustave Eiffel, CNRS

ru.title: Универсальный кодировщик для трансформеров земного зондирования
ru.desc: UniverSat предлагает Universal Patch Encoder для Vision Transformers, который решает проблему жёсткой привязки к входным данным в задачах дистанционного зондирования Земли. Модель использует единое пространство эмбеддингов для работы с данными различных разрешений, спектральных характеристик и датчиков (оптических и неоптических). Обучение проводится на разнородных мультимодальных корпусах с помощью самообучения, что позволяет извлекать универсальные и устойчивые пространственные признаки. Подход достигает сильных результатов в задачах классификации и сегментации на стандартных бенчмарках дистанционного зондирования.

en.title: UniverSat: A Universal Solution for Earth Observation with Vision Transformers
en.desc: UniverSat presents a Universal Patch Encoder designed for Vision Transformers, enhancing their ability to extract spatial features from various Earth Observation (EO) data types. Traditional Vision Transformers struggle with the diverse input modalities and resolutions found in EO data due to their fixed patch projectors. The Universal Patch Encoder allows for flexible mapping of patches from different spatial, spectral, and temporal resolutions into a unified embedding space. This innovation enables a single model to be trained on diverse multimodal datasets using self-supervision, resulting in robust and sensor-agnostic features that perform well in classification and segmentation tasks.

zh.title: 通用补丁编码器：跨模态特征提取的未来
zh.desc: UniverSat 是一种通用补丁编码器，专为视觉变换器设计，能够在不同的地球观测数据类型中进行稳健的空间特征提取。该模型克服了传统补丁投影器的局限性，支持多种输入模态、尺度和分辨率。通过自监督学习，UniverSat 可以在异构多模态数据集上训练单一模型，从而生成传感器无关的空间特征。我们在多个标准地球观测基准上验证了该方法，取得了良好的分类和分割结果。

papers.title: Demystifying Training-Time Augmentation for Data-Constrained Language Model Pretraining
papers.abstract: Abstract Training-time data augmentation techniques help mitigate overfitting in autoregressive language model pretraining by delaying performance deterioration and improving final model quality when training on fixed datasets for many epochs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As AI labs approach a data ceiling where compute capacity outpaces the rate of new high-quality text generation, language model pretraining is shifting toward a data-constrained, compute-abundant regime that demands productive multi-epoch training on fixed corpora. Standard autoregressive (AR) pretraining overfits severely in this setting, reaching its optimum early and then continuously deteriorating. We investigate training-time data augmentation as a regularizer to mitigate this overfitting and enable productive training for hundreds of epochs on the same data. We introduce three orthogonal categories of augmentation for AR pretraining: token-level noise (masking, random replacement), sequence permutations (right-to-left prediction, Fill-in-the-Middle), and target offset prediction (x_{t+i} for i > 1). Through systematic ablations, we find that individual augmentations delay overfitting and lower validation loss relative to the baseline, with random token replacement achieving the best minimum loss among individual methods. Combining augmentation categories further lowers the minimum validation loss. Our experiments demonstrate that data augmentations mitigate AR pretraining's data inefficiency and offer a promising solution to the data-constrained regime~\footnote{All code and data are available at https://github.com/ michaelchen-lab/ data-augmentations-for-pretraining.

ru.title: Аугментация данных как решение переобучения при многоэпохном обучении языковых моделей
ru.desc: В статье исследуются методы аугментации данных во время обучения для предварительной подготовки автоregрессивных языковых моделей. Авторы предлагают три категории аугментации: шумирование на уровне токенов (маскирование и случайная замена), перестановка последовательностей (предсказание справа налево, Fill-in-the-Middle) и предсказание со смещением целевой позиции. Через систематические абляционные исследования показано, что комбинирование различных методов аугментации эффективно снижает потери валидации и задерживает переобучение при многократном обучении на одном и том же корпусе. Предложенный подход решает проблему неэффективности использования данных в условиях ограниченного размера датасета и избыточной вычислительной мощности.

en.title: Boosting Language Models: Data Augmentation to Combat Overfitting
en.desc: This paper explores how training-time data augmentation can help autoregressive language models avoid overfitting during pretraining. By applying techniques like token-level noise, sequence permutations, and target offset prediction, the authors show that these augmentations can improve model performance over many training epochs on fixed datasets. The study finds that individual augmentation methods, particularly random token replacement, effectively reduce validation loss. Additionally, combining different augmentation strategies leads to even better results, making it a valuable approach in data-constrained training environments.

zh.title: 数据增强：提升自回归模型训练效果的关键
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在自回归语言模型预训练中使用训练时数据增强技术，以减轻过拟合现象。通过引入三种不同类型的数据增强方法，包括标记级噪声、序列排列和目标偏移预测，研究发现这些方法能够有效延迟过拟合并降低验证损失。实验结果表明，随机标记替换在单一方法中表现最佳，而组合多种增强方法则进一步降低了验证损失。总的来说，这些数据增强技术为在数据受限的环境中进行有效的多轮训练提供了有希望的解决方案。

papers.title: CalVerT: Augmenting Agents with Calibrated Verifier Telemetry Improves Action and Learning in Knowledge-Intensive Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract Calibrated verifier telemetry enhances LLM agents in knowledge-intensive question answering by providing confidence scores and grounding verification, reducing both over-retrieval and unsupported answers.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM agents in knowledge intensive question answering take retrieval and reasoning actions with incomplete knowledge about whether their current answer is uncertain, unsupported, or already complete. This produces two failure modes: committing to confident but unsupported answers, which hurts accuracy, and over-retrieving when the evidence in hand already suffices, resulting in wasted compute. To give agents a more complete picture of the state space they are operating in, we introduce calibrated verifier telemetry (CalVerT), which augments the agent's state with additional telemetry: a calibrated self-confidence score and a grounding verifier score. We show that CalVerT can improve agents in both training-free and training-based settings. On four QA benchmarks, we find that CalVerT raises F1 by triggering retrieval in cases where agents over-rely on parametric knowledge, while cutting redundant retrieval in cases where agents have sufficient context to answer. We show that CalVerT can augment existing QA frameworks without training. Moreover, CalVerT also improves trained systems: by simply augmenting an agent's state with telemetry, we observe improvements after reinforcement learning, as compared to an agent with identical training but no CalVerT telemetry.

ru.title: Телеметрия верификатора для надёжных ответов агентов LLM
ru.desc: В работе предлагается метод калиброванной телеметрии верификатора (CalVerT) для улучшения агентов на основе LLM при решении задач вопросно-ответного поиска в условиях неполной информации. Метод добавляет к состоянию агента два сигнала: откалиброванный скор уверенности и скор проверки обоснованности, что позволяет устранить два типа ошибок — чрезмерно уверенные необоснованные ответы и избыточный поиск информации. CalVerT показывает улучшения как в режиме без обучения, так и при использовании обучения с подкреплением, повышая метрику F1 на четырёх бенчмарках за счёт более точного управления процессом поиска документов. Предложенный подход может быть интегрирован в существующие системы вопросно-ответного поиска без дополнительного обучения модели.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Agents with Calibrated Confidence for Better QA
en.desc: This paper introduces Calibrated Verifier Telemetry (CalVerT) to enhance large language model (LLM) agents in knowledge-intensive question answering tasks. CalVerT provides agents with a calibrated self-confidence score and a grounding verifier score, helping them assess the reliability of their answers. By reducing over-retrieval and unsupported answers, CalVerT improves the accuracy of the agents' responses. The results show that CalVerT can be integrated into existing QA frameworks without additional training, leading to better performance on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 校准验证器遥测提升问答系统的准确性
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为校准验证器遥测（CalVerT）的技术，旨在增强大型语言模型（LLM）在知识密集型问答中的表现。通过提供自信度评分和基础验证评分，CalVerT帮助代理更好地理解其回答的可靠性，从而减少错误回答和过度检索的情况。研究表明，CalVerT在多个问答基准测试中提高了F1分数，尤其是在代理过度依赖参数知识时，能够有效触发检索，同时在已有足够上下文的情况下减少冗余检索。该技术可以在不进行训练的情况下增强现有的问答框架，并且在经过强化学习后也能显著提升训练系统的表现。

papers.title: TROPT: An Open Framework for Unifying and Advancing Discrete Text Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified open-source framework for discrete text-trigger optimization that standardizes the development and execution of optimization strategies across various domains and applications.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Discrete text-trigger optimization -- searching for text sequences that, when ingested by a model, steer it toward a specified objective -- underpins model red-teaming (e.g., LLM jailbreaks), as well as auditing and interpretability. However, the current state of discrete optimizers hinders their adoption and progress. First, existing optimizers, when open-sourced at all, are scattered across research codebases tied to specific models, objectives, and problem domains. Second, optimizer variants proliferate, each requiring engineering overhead to use or extend, and remaining hard to compare head-to-head. Together, these raise the bar for adopting optimizers in existing or new domains, and for advancing them via new strategies. We address these gaps with TROPT, the first open-source framework that unifies discrete optimizers' execution and standardizes their development under a single interface. TROPT makes it easy to customize end-to-end optimization recipes by swapping any component -- models, objectives, and optimizers -- extending its reach across domains and new applications. TROPT currently ships with 30+ optimization recipes -- covering applications such as jailbreaking and probing model internals -- built from 15+ optimizers (spanning white-box to black-box access) and 15+ losses, from foundational to state-of-the-art methods. Demonstrating its utility, we leverage TROPT in several studies: (i) controlled, large-scale experiments comparing and enhancing optimization strategies for LLM jailbreaks, revealing potent-yet-underadopted techniques; and (ii) porting optimizers from one domain (e.g., LLM jailbreak) to new domains (e.g., corpus-poisoning embedding model). In all, TROPT significantly lowers the barrier to adopting and advancing discrete text optimization.

ru.title: Универсальный фреймворк для поиска оптимальных текстовых атак
ru.desc: В статье представлен TROPT — единый фреймворк с открытым исходным кодом для оптимизации дискретных текстовых триггеров, который унифицирует разработку и выполнение стратегий оптимизации. Фреймворк решает проблему рассеянности существующих оптимизаторов по разным кодовым базам и позволяет легко комбинировать компоненты: модели, целевые функции и алгоритмы оптимизации. TROPT включает более 30 готовых рецептов оптимизации, построенных из 15+ оптимизаторов (от белого ящика до чёрного) и 15+ функций потерь, применимых в разных доменах, включая jailbreak'инг LLM и анализ внутреннего устройства моделей. Авторы демонстрируют практическую ценность фреймворка через масштабные экспериментальные исследования и успешную адаптацию оптимизаторов между разными задачами.

en.title: TROPT: Unifying Discrete Text Optimization for All
en.desc: This paper introduces TROPT, an open-source framework designed to streamline discrete text-trigger optimization across various applications. Discrete text-trigger optimization involves finding specific text sequences that guide models towards desired outcomes, which is crucial for tasks like model auditing and red-teaming. The framework addresses the fragmentation of existing optimizers and simplifies their use by providing a unified interface for customization and comparison. With over 30 optimization recipes and support for multiple optimizers and loss functions, TROPT enhances accessibility and encourages innovation in the field of discrete optimization.

zh.title: 统一离散文本优化，简化模型开发
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个统一的开源框架TROPT，用于离散文本触发优化，旨在标准化不同领域和应用中的优化策略开发与执行。离散文本触发优化是指寻找文本序列，以引导模型朝向特定目标，这在模型安全性测试和可解释性中至关重要。当前的离散优化器分散且难以比较，限制了其在新领域的应用和进步。TROPT通过提供一个统一接口，简化了优化过程，使得用户可以轻松定制优化方案，并支持多种模型和目标的组合。

papers.title: When Agents Commit Too Soon: Diagnosing Premature Commitment in LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Pre premature commitment in long-horizon LLM agents leads to silent failures where agents defend early interpretations without considering alternatives, and hidden-state convergence serves as an early diagnostic for trajectory consistency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long-horizon LLM agents can fail quietly: they settle on one reading of the evidence early, then spend the rest of the run defending it. We call this premature commitment. Final-answer scoring misses the failure mode because it sees only the answer, not whether the process has already collapsed to a stable path. We define representational commitment as cross-run hidden-state convergence at a fixed reasoning step, and use it as an early diagnostic of trajectory consistency. On Llama-3.1-70B running ReAct on HotpotQA, step-4 hidden-state similarity predicts downstream behavioral consistency (r = -0.35, partial r = -0.45), with a localized temporal and layer-wise signature. The signal replicates across Qwen-2.5-72B and Phi-3-14B, and on StrategyQA (r = -0.83). It does not track correctness: committed-wrong and committed-correct questions are not separable in activation similarity. That boundary is central to the claim. Commitment tells us whether an agent has settled, not whether it is right. A runtime monitor detects inconsistent trajectories from hidden states at AUROC up to 0.97 (0.85--0.88 under a stricter split), and a prompting intervention cuts behavioral variance by 28% against a token-matched control while leaving accuracy statistically unchanged. We also test whether the signal can route self-consistency compute; on a harder benchmark it helps only modestly and is matched by a simpler output-based baseline. The result is a diagnostic for a hidden process failure, with clear limits rather than a general accuracy lever.

ru.title: Ловушка раннего решения: как обнаружить застревание мысли в LLM-агентах
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема преждевременной приверженности в долгосрочных агентах на основе LLM, когда модели слишком рано выбирают интерпретацию и затем защищают её вместо рассмотрения альтернатив. Авторы предлагают использовать сходимость скрытых состояний между прогонами как ранний диагностический признак для обнаружения стабилизации траектории рассуждений. Экспериментально показано, что подобие скрытых состояний на определённых шагах вычисления предсказывает консистентность поведения агента, однако это не коррелирует с корректностью ответов. На основе этого сигнала разработан монитор во время выполнения, который детектирует несогласованные траектории с высокой точностью и позволяет частично снизить дисперсию поведения через подсказки.

en.title: Detecting Premature Commitment in LLMs for Better Decision-Making
en.desc: This paper discusses the issue of premature commitment in long-horizon large language model (LLM) agents, where they settle on an interpretation too early and fail to consider other possibilities. The authors introduce the concept of representational commitment, which is identified through hidden-state convergence at specific reasoning steps, serving as a diagnostic tool for trajectory consistency. They demonstrate that this hidden-state similarity can predict behavioral consistency in LLMs, even when it does not correlate with the correctness of the answers. The findings suggest that monitoring hidden states can help identify when an agent has prematurely committed to a path, providing insights into the internal processes of LLMs.

zh.title: 早期承诺：长时间跨度LLM代理的隐性失败
zh.desc: 本文探讨了长时间跨度的语言模型（LLM）代理在早期承诺时可能出现的静默失败现象。这种现象称为“早期承诺”，代理在早期就对证据形成了固定的理解，之后只会捍卫这一理解，而不考虑其他可能性。我们定义了表示承诺为在固定推理步骤下的跨运行隐藏状态收敛，并将其作为轨迹一致性的早期诊断工具。研究表明，隐藏状态的相似性可以有效预测后续行为的一致性，尽管它并不能直接反映答案的正确性。

papers.title: Capable but Careless: Do Computer-Use Agents Follow Contextual Integrity?
papers.abstract: Abstract Computer-use agents frequently expose inappropriate information across applications, prompting the creation of AgentCIBench to evaluate and mitigate privacy risks in cross-application contexts.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Computer-use agents (CUAs) now act on a user's behalf across personal applications such as email, calendars, and to-do lists. This cross-application access is useful, but it also creates a privacy risk that has been largely overlooked: when an agent works in one context, it can pull in information from another that is inappropriate in that context. Hence, we introduce AgentCIBench, an evaluation harness that turns this risk into executable, deterministically scored scenarios. We target three common failure modes in CUAs: visual co-location, where the agent pulls in prohibited items that sit next to the task target in the UI; task-ambiguity overshare, where the agent dumps dense personal state in response to an under-specified prompt; and recipient misalignment, where the agent sends content to an addressee for whom it is inappropriate. We evaluate 15 frontier agents and find a surprisingly high failure rate: 11 of 15 leak on more than 50% of scenarios, with an average leakage of 67.9%, and the same failures persist when agents act end-to-end in the environment to complete the task. We release AgentCIBench to encourage the development of safer computer-use agents and position contextual disclosure testing as a pre-deployment safety check.
affiliations: National Research Center for Applied Cybersecurity ATHENE
Ubiquitous Knowledge Processing Lab (UKP Lab), Department of Computer Science, TU Darmstadt

ru.title: Оценка и снижение рисков утечки приватных данных в мультиприложенческих AI-агентах
ru.desc: В работе представлена AgentCIBench — бенчмарк для оценки рисков утечки приватной информации в агентах, которые взаимодействуют с несколькими приложениями одновременно. Авторы выявили три основных типа ошибок в компьютерных агентах: визуальная соприкосновенность (когда агент извлекает запрещённые данные рядом с целевым объектом), чрезмерная выдача информации при неточных запросах и неправильное выбор адресата сообщения. Тестирование 15 передовых моделей показало тревожно высокий уровень утечек — 11 из 15 агентов пропускают более 50% сценариев с средней утечкой 67.9%. Авторы предлагают AgentCIBench как инструмент для разработки более безопасных агентов и проведения проверок безопасности перед развёртыванием.

en.title: Mitigating Privacy Risks in Cross-Application Agents
en.desc: This paper introduces AgentCIBench, a tool designed to evaluate and reduce privacy risks associated with computer-use agents (CUAs) that operate across multiple applications. CUAs can inadvertently expose sensitive information by accessing data from different contexts, leading to privacy breaches. The study identifies three main failure modes where CUAs can leak inappropriate information: visual co-location, task-ambiguity overshare, and recipient misalignment. The evaluation of 15 advanced agents revealed a significant leakage issue, with many agents failing to protect user privacy in over half of the tested scenarios, highlighting the need for improved safety measures in their deployment.

zh.title: 提升计算机使用代理的隐私安全性
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了AgentCIBench，这是一个用于评估和减轻跨应用程序隐私风险的工具。计算机使用代理（CUA）在多个个人应用程序中操作，但这种跨应用访问可能导致隐私泄露。我们识别了三种常见的失败模式，包括视觉共存、任务模糊过度共享和接收者不匹配。通过对15个前沿代理的评估，我们发现大多数代理在超过50%的场景中存在信息泄露，平均泄露率达到67.9%。

papers.title: Arbor: Explicit Geometric Conditioning for Controllable 3D Asset Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Arbor enables explicit 3D spatial control in text-conditioned latent generation through constraint meshes that define occupancy, avoidance, and contact regions, maintaining object quality while improving constraint adherence.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Text and image conditioned 3D models now generate convincing assets, but they still offer little direct control over the space an object should occupy or avoid. In authoring, this spatial intent is often known before generation starts. A chair should fit a seating envelope, a prop should leave clearance for motion, or a part should expose a contact surface. Prompts and image views are poor carriers for such constraints, requiring the need for an explicit control interface.   We present Arbor, a trainable attachment for text conditioned latent 3D generation. Arbor introduces constraint meshes as a native 3D control interface. The interface uses hull regions where geometry should exist, avoidance regions that should remain empty, and touch regions the object should contact. Unlike completion or whole object scaffold control, these meshes are not target evidence. They are local typed requirements and can include regions where no surface should appear. Arbor keeps this signal as geometry by converting constraint meshes into tokens and learning a routed attachment inside a frozen denoiser. Each latent region can therefore receive the part of the constraint that matters for its spatial location.   We evaluate Arbor on automatic and artist curated control benchmarks with hull, avoidance, and touch constraints, and compare the metric trends to a user preference study. Even without dedicated compliance losses, Arbor improves constraint obedience while preserving object quality and variation under fixed constraints.

ru.title: Явный пространственный контроль в 3D-генерации через сетки ограничений
ru.desc: Arbor — это обучаемый модуль для текстово-обусловленной генерации 3D объектов в латентном пространстве, который позволяет явно контролировать пространственное расположение генерируемых моделей через сетки ограничений. Система использует три типа регионов: области занятости (где должна быть геометрия), области избегания (где геометрии быть не должно) и области контакта (где объект должен взаимодействовать с окружением). Ключевое отличие подхода состоит в том, что сетки ограничений преобразуются в токены и интегрируются в замороженный денойзер через маршрутизируемый модуль, позволяя каждому латентному региону получать релевантную информацию о ограничениях. Эксперименты демонстрируют, что Arbor улучшает соответствие ограничениям при сохранении качества и разнообразия генерируемых объектов без использования специальных loss-функций.

en.title: Arbor: Control 3D Generation with Spatial Constraints
