en.desc: The paper presents Arbor, a novel tool for generating 3D models that allows users to control the spatial properties of objects through constraint meshes. These meshes define specific areas where objects should exist, avoid, or make contact, enhancing the quality and adherence to spatial requirements during the generation process. Unlike traditional methods that rely on prompts or images, Arbor provides a direct interface for specifying spatial intent before the generation begins. The evaluation shows that Arbor improves the adherence to these constraints while maintaining the quality and diversity of the generated 3D assets.

zh.title: Arbor：实现3D生成的空间控制新方法
zh.desc: Arbor 是一种用于文本条件下的潜在 3D 生成的可训练附加组件，能够实现明确的 3D 空间控制。它通过约束网格定义了占用、避免和接触区域，从而在保持物体质量的同时提高约束遵循性。与传统的生成方法不同，Arbor 提供了一种本地的 3D 控制接口，使得用户可以在生成之前明确空间意图。通过将约束网格转换为标记，Arbor 能够在固定的去噪器中学习路由附加，从而在潜在区域中接收与其空间位置相关的约束信息。

papers.title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract MeshFlow generates triangle meshes directly using equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models with improved inference speed over autoregressive methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Meshes are among the most common 3D scene representations, but directly generating meshes is challenging because the representation contains important symmetries, including permutation invariance of faces and vertices. MeshFlow learns to generate triangle meshes directly as triangle soups, avoiding the need to serialize meshes into long autoregressive sequences. We adopt equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models that respect the key symmetries of triangle soups: arbitrary permutations of faces and permutations of the vertices within each face.   Toward this goal, we propose a simple yet effective modification to the Diffusion Transformer architecture, resulting in a scalable network capable of modeling a velocity field while maintaining the desired equivariance. We further introduce an optimal-transport-based training objective that improves convergence by eliminating supervision signals that violate these symmetries. MeshFlow achieves mesh quality comparable to state-of-the-art autoregressive mesh generators while providing about an 18times speedup during inference. Project page is at https://qiisun.github.io/MeshFlow/.

ru.title: Быстрое прямое генерирование 3D-сеток через эквивариантное flow matching
ru.desc: MeshFlow — это модель генеративного моделирования, которая создаёт треугольные сетки напрямую, используя flow matching с учётом эквивариантности относительно перестановок вершин и граней. В отличие от авторегрессивных подходов, модель избегает сериализации сеток в длинные последовательности, работая непосредственно с облаками треугольников. Авторы предложили модификацию архитектуры Diffusion Transformer, которая моделирует поле скоростей при сохранении необходимых симметрий представления. Использование оптимального транспорта в качестве целевой функции обучения значительно улучшает качество генерации и обеспечивает ускорение вывода примерно в 18 раз по сравнению с автор егрессивными методами.

en.title: Fast and Symmetric Mesh Generation with MeshFlow
en.desc: MeshFlow is a novel approach for generating triangle meshes using equivariant optimal-transport flow matching models, which significantly enhances inference speed compared to traditional autoregressive methods. The method addresses the complexities of mesh representation, particularly the symmetries such as permutation invariance of faces and vertices. By generating triangle meshes directly as triangle soups, MeshFlow eliminates the need for lengthy autoregressive sequences. The architecture incorporates a modified Diffusion Transformer and an optimal-transport-based training objective, achieving high-quality mesh generation with a remarkable 18 times faster inference speed.

zh.title: MeshFlow：快速生成三角网格的创新方法
zh.desc: MeshFlow 是一种直接生成三角网格的模型，使用等变最优传输流匹配方法，推理速度比自回归方法快。三角网格是常见的三维场景表示，但直接生成网格很具挑战性，因为它包含重要的对称性。MeshFlow 学习直接生成三角网格，避免将网格序列化为长的自回归序列。我们提出了一种简单有效的 Diffusion Transformer 架构修改，使网络能够建模速度场，同时保持所需的等变性。

papers.title: HAKARI-Bench: A Lightweight Benchmark for Comparing Retrieval Architectures and Efficiency Settings under Unified Conditions
papers.abstract: Abstract HAKARI-Bench provides a lightweight benchmark for comparing retrieval methods across multiple configurations and languages, enabling efficient model selection and performance analysis.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct With the rapid spread of retrieval-augmented generation and semantic search, choosing the right embedding and retrieval configuration is increasingly hard. Large retrieval benchmarks are comprehensive but too heavy to rerun during development, and there is little infrastructure for comparing production settings--dimensionality reduction, quantization, reranking--across many models under identical conditions. We present HAKARI-Bench, a lightweight benchmark that reconstructs existing retrieval suites into small datasets (Nano-sets): 35 benchmarks and 551 tasks across 43 languages in a unified format, enabling same-condition, model-agnostic comparison of five retrieval families (BM25, dense, sparse, late interaction, rerankers) and their efficiency variants. Across 55 models, its overall ranking reproduces the official MTEB retrieval v2, MMTEB v2 retrieval, and English BEIR (full) at Spearman >0.97. HAKARI-Bench does not replace full evaluation; it enables rapid model selection, regression detection, and reading the quality-efficiency Pareto frontier. Code, data, and leaderboard are released under the MIT license.

ru.title: Быстрый выбор лучшей модели поиска без тяжёлых вычислений
ru.desc: HAKARI-Bench — это лёгкий бенчмарк для сравнения методов поиска и ранжирования документов на множестве конфигураций и языков. Авторы сжали существующие большие наборы данных в компактные датасеты, охватывающие 35 бенчмарков и 551 задач на 43 языках, позволяя быстро тестировать пять семейств retrieval-методов (BM25, плотные представления, разреженные представления, late interaction и rerankers) в унифицированном формате. Бенчмарк валидирован на 55 моделях и показывает высокую корреляцию (>0.97) с полными оценками MTEB и BEIR. Инструмент предназначен для быстрого отбора моделей, обнаружения регрессий и анализа компромисса между качеством и эффективностью.

en.title: Streamlined Retrieval Comparison with HAKARI-Bench
en.desc: HAKARI-Bench is a new lightweight benchmark designed to simplify the comparison of various retrieval methods across different configurations and languages. It addresses the challenges of selecting the right embedding and retrieval settings in the context of retrieval-augmented generation and semantic search. By creating small datasets called Nano-sets, HAKARI-Bench allows for efficient, model-agnostic evaluations of multiple retrieval families under consistent conditions. This tool facilitates quick model selection and performance analysis while maintaining high correlation with established benchmarks.

zh.title: 轻量级检索基准，助力模型选择与性能分析
zh.desc: HAKARI-Bench 是一个轻量级基准，用于比较多种配置和语言下的检索方法，帮助高效选择模型和分析性能。随着检索增强生成和语义搜索的快速发展，选择合适的嵌入和检索配置变得越来越困难。HAKARI-Bench 将现有的检索套件重构为小型数据集（Nano-sets），提供了 35 个基准和 551 个任务，支持在相同条件下对五种检索家族进行模型无关的比较。该基准的整体排名与官方的 MTEB 检索 v2 和其他基准高度一致，能够快速进行模型选择和回归检测。

papers.title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
papers.abstract: Abstract Lift4D presents a test-time optimization framework that combines temporal consistency from single-view 3D reconstruction with deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting and view-conditioned diffusion priors to reconstruct dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reconstructing dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video requires integrating visual cues from direct observations with data-driven priors over geometry and appearance. Prior approaches either learn to directly predict 4D representations from visual input or initialize a 3D representation that is subsequently deformed and refined based on video evidence. However, the former are constrained by the scarcity of 4D training data, while the latter leverage priors only for the initial reconstruction and rely solely on video supervision thereafter; neither handles complex in-the-wild scenarios with large deformations and occlusions well. We present Lift4D, a test-time optimization framework that addresses both limitations. First, we adapt an existing single-view 3D reconstruction model to yield temporally consistent per-frame predictions via causal latent conditioning, providing a coherent initialization for a deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting representation. We then ``sculpt'' this representation to match the input video through an occlusion-aware optimization that faithfully recovers visible surface details while completing unobserved regions using a view-conditioned diffusion prior. We demonstrate that Lift4D clearly improves over prior 4D reconstruction methods, particularly on challenging in-the-wild sequences with severe occlusions and non-rigid motion.

ru.title: Лифтинг 2D видео в динамическую 4D геометрию через оптимизацию во время тестирования
ru.desc: Lift4D представляет фреймворк оптимизации на этапе тестирования для реконструкции динамических деформируемых объектов из видео с одной камеры. Метод объединяет временную согласованность от модели 3D реконструкции с деформируемым 3D Gaussian Splatting и диффузионными приорами, обусловленными точкой зрения. Авторы решают проблему дефицита 4D тренировочных данных, используя каузальную латентную кондиционировку для получения согласованных предсказаний по кадрам, а затем оптимизируют представление с учетом окклюзий для восстановления видимых деталей и заполнения скрытых областей. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение в реконструкции сложных сцен с сильными окклюзиями и нежесткой деформацией.

en.title: Revolutionizing 4D Reconstruction with Lift4D
en.desc: Lift4D introduces a novel framework for optimizing the reconstruction of dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video. It combines temporal consistency from single-view 3D reconstruction with deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting and view-conditioned diffusion priors. This approach addresses the limitations of previous methods by providing a coherent initialization and refining the 3D representation through occlusion-aware optimization. The results show significant improvements in handling complex scenarios with large deformations and occlusions compared to earlier 4D reconstruction techniques.

zh.title: Lift4D：动态非刚性物体重建的新方法
zh.desc: Lift4D是一个测试时优化框架，旨在从单目视频中重建动态非刚性物体。它结合了单视图3D重建的时间一致性、可变形3D高斯点云和视图条件扩散先验。该方法通过因果潜在条件化，提供了一致的初始化，随后通过考虑遮挡的优化来雕刻3D表示，以匹配输入视频。实验表明，Lift4D在处理复杂场景时，尤其是严重遮挡和非刚性运动方面，明显优于之前的4D重建方法。

papers.title: Libretto: Giving LLM Agents a Sense of Musical Structure
papers.abstract: Abstract Libretto provides a structured framework for symbolic music generation and revision using LLM-native grammar and statistical evaluation across musical dimensions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Generative music systems can now produce impressive audio from text prompts, but audio outputs are difficult to inspect, edit, and diagnose as musical structure. We introduce Libretto, an agent-facing framework for symbolic music generation and revision. Libretto uses an LLM-native grammar with explicit onset slots, voices, and bar-level organization, then evaluates each piece in a corpus-calibrated statistical space over rhythm, harmony, melody, texture, form, and variation. The same structural axes support retrieval, diagnosis, copy-risk control, and iterative self-revision. Across gap filling, reference-guided full-piece generation, gradual morphing, and educational music generation, Libretto turns symbolic music from a raw token sequence into a measurable and editable object for language-model agents.

ru.title: Музыка как редактируемая структура: от токенов к измеримым объектам
ru.desc: Libretto — это фреймворк для генерации и редактирования символической музыки с использованием LLM, построенный на специальной грамматике, которая явно представляет временные позиции, голоса и структуру произведения. Система оценивает сгенерированную музыку в многомерном статистическом пространстве по шести музыкальным характеристикам: ритму, гармонии, мелодии, фактуре, форме и вариативности, калибруя оценки на корпусе реальных произведений. Эти же структурные параметры используются для поиска, диагностики проблем, контроля плагиата и итеративного самоулучшения музыки агентом LLM. Подход преобразует музыкальное произведение из простой последовательности токенов в измеримый и редактируемый объект, который можно контролировать и совершенствовать.

en.title: Transforming Music Generation with Structured Symbolic Frameworks
en.desc: Libretto is a framework designed for generating and revising symbolic music using large language models (LLMs). It incorporates a grammar that organizes music into structured components like onset slots and voices, allowing for better manipulation of musical elements. The framework evaluates music based on various statistical dimensions such as rhythm, harmony, and melody, making it easier to analyze and improve compositions. By transforming music into a measurable format, Libretto enables tasks like gap filling and educational music generation, enhancing the capabilities of generative music systems.

zh.title: Libretto：音乐生成的新框架
zh.desc: Libretto是一个用于符号音乐生成和修订的框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）原生语法和统计评估来处理音乐的各个维度。它通过明确的起始音符、声部和小节级组织来生成音乐，并在节奏、和声、旋律、音色、形式和变化等方面进行统计评估。Libretto支持检索、诊断、复制风险控制和迭代自我修订，使得音乐生成过程更加可控和可编辑。通过填补空白、参考引导的完整作品生成、渐进变形和教育音乐生成，Libretto将符号音乐转化为可测量和可编辑的对象。

papers.title: Go-with-the-Track: Video Compositing and Motion Control with Point Tracking
papers.abstract: Abstract Go-with-the-Track unifies motion control and reference image compositing in video generation by using point-track embeddings with spatial-aware encoding and video diffusion transformers.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Filmmaking demands precise motion control and reference image compositing -- capabilities that existing methods treat separately. Point-track-conditioned image-to-video models restrict content insertion to the first frame, while reference-to-video models lack fine-grained spatial-temporal control over how reference content integrates across frames.   We present Go-with-the-Track, which unifies both capabilities by jointly conditioning on multiple reference images and reference-anchored point-tracks -- extending conventional point-tracks to explicitly establish correspondences between generated frames and reference images, thus enabling precise compositing and motion control throughout the video.   To achieve this, we introduce spatially-aware point-track embeddings that encode the full sequence of point-track coordinates using a coordinate-wise MLP followed by temporal pooling. This representation captures the spatial characteristics of each point-track (serving as a unique identifier), while the embedding similarity correlates directly with spatial proximity, enhancing the model's ability to distinguish and associate point-tracks. We inject these point-track embeddings into a video diffusion transformer via a lightweight adapter, resolving the pixel-to-patch resolution mismatch while avoiding the substantial motion detail loss inherent in naive point-track subsampling.   We use a hybrid training strategy to train jointly on dynamic, static, and synthetic scene video datasets to boost motion controllability. Experiments demonstrate that Go-with-the-Track achieves superior motion and reference control in a single model and enables new capabilities: multi-reference conditioned video generation with point-track driven compositing, as well as camera control for both static and dynamic scenes. Project Page: https://eyeline-labs.github.io/Go-with-the-Track/
affiliations: University of California, Los Angeles, USA

ru.title: Единое управление движением и композицией в видеогенерации через point-track embeddings
ru.desc: В этой работе предложен метод Go-with-the-Track, который объединяет управление движением и композицию видео с использованием reference изображений через точечные траектории. Авторы вводят пространственно-осведомленные embeddings точечных треков, которые кодируют последовательность координат с помощью координатных MLP-сетей и temporal pooling. Эти embeddings встраиваются в видеодиффузионный transformer через легкий адаптер, что позволяет избежать потери деталей движения при наивном subsampling. Гибридная стратегия обучения на динамических, статичных и синтетических видео позволяет достичь точного контроля над движением и композицией в одной модели.

en.title: Unifying Motion Control and Image Compositing in Video Generation
en.desc: The paper presents Go-with-the-Track, a novel approach that combines motion control and reference image compositing for video generation. It utilizes point-track embeddings with spatial-aware encoding and video diffusion transformers to enhance the integration of reference content across frames. By extending conventional point-tracks, the method allows for precise compositing and motion control throughout the video, addressing limitations of existing models. The hybrid training strategy employed improves motion controllability, enabling advanced features like multi-reference conditioned video generation and camera control in various scenes.

zh.title: 统一运动控制与图像合成的创新方法
zh.desc: Go-with-the-Track 是一种视频生成方法，它将运动控制和参考图像合成结合在一起。该方法使用空间感知的点轨迹嵌入和视频扩散变换器，能够在整个视频中实现精确的合成和运动控制。通过联合条件化多个参考图像和参考锚定的点轨迹，Go-with-the-Track 解决了传统方法在内容插入和时空控制上的局限。实验表明，该方法在运动和参考控制方面表现优越，并支持多参考条件的视频生成。

papers.title: Robusto-2: Benchmarking Humans & VLMs for Autonomous Driving in Lima & New York City
papers.abstract: Abstract Research examines how self-driving car systems and humans perform on visual question answering tasks across different geographic locations, revealing that both human and AI responses diverge based on question types but show similar performance regardless of location.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As Self-Driving Cars continue to expand internationally and use multi-modal systems such as VLMs as a cognitive backbone for their Action models; how well will these systems generalize in new settings, in particular out-of-distribution (OOD) edge-case scenarios in new geographies? In this paper, we study this open question by providing a full factorial analysis with human drivers of Lima, human drivers from New York City, and VLMs and showing them dashcam footage collected from Lima and New York City -- prompting them with a variety of questions under a Visual Question Answering (VQA) paradigm. In particular, we pick these two cities as they are highly challenging driving locations where no Self-Driving Car company currently operates in, and ask questions that span 4 categories: Factual, Ratings, Counterfactual and Reasoning. We find that Humans and VLMs diverge in their responses -- though this is modulated by the type of questions asked, and that Humans answer similarly independent of where they are from (Lima/NYC). To our surprise, we did not find a strong difference in terms of answers (Humans or VLMs) that was modulated by geography, likely due to their high out-of-distribution nature. Our dataset is available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Artificio/robusto-2

ru.title: Географическая обобщаемость визуально-языковых моделей для автономных автомобилей
ru.desc: Исследование изучает, как системы автономного вождения и люди справляются с задачей ответов на визуальные вопросы в различных географических локациях. Авторы проводят факторный анализ, сравнивая ответы водителей из Лимы и Нью-Йорка с результатами визуально-языковых моделей (VLM) на видеозаписях с видеорегистраторов. Они обнаруживают, что люди и VLM дают разные ответы в зависимости от типа вопроса, но географическое происхождение водителей не влияет на их результаты. Исследование показывает, что высокая степень распределения вне тренировочных данных затрудняет обобщение моделей на новые географические регионы, что важно для развития безопасных систем автономного вождения.

en.title: Exploring VQA Performance of Humans and AI in Diverse Driving Environments
en.desc: This paper investigates how self-driving car systems and human drivers perform on visual question answering (VQA) tasks in different geographic locations. It compares responses from human drivers in Lima and New York City, as well as from visual language models (VLMs), using dashcam footage and a variety of question types. The study finds that while both humans and VLMs show different response patterns based on question types, their performance does not significantly vary by location. This suggests that the challenges posed by out-of-distribution scenarios may not be as influenced by geographic factors as previously thought.

zh.title: 自驾车与人类在视觉问答中的表现对比
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了自驾车系统和人类在视觉问答任务中的表现，发现两者的回答在问题类型上存在差异，但无论地理位置如何，表现相似。我们通过对来自利马和纽约市的人类驾驶员及视觉语言模型（VLMs）进行全面分析，展示了他们在不同城市的行车记录视频上回答问题的情况。研究表明，尽管人类和VLMs的回答存在差异，但这种差异受问题类型的影响，而人类的回答与其地理来源无关。令人惊讶的是，我们没有发现地理位置对回答的显著影响，这可能是由于高出分布特性所致。

papers.title: AC-ODM: Actor--Critic Online Data Mixing for Sample-Efficient LLM Pretraining
papers.abstract: Abstract AC-ODM optimizes pretraining data composition for LLMs using reinforcement learning to improve convergence speed and downstream accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Optimizing pretraining data composition is pivotal for LLM generalization. While dynamic mixing outperforms static strategies by capturing evolving training dynamics, current methods fail to reconcile computational efficiency with sample efficiency and structural flexibility for diverse pipelines.We introduce Actor--Critic Online Data Mixing (AC-ODM), which approaches data mixing from a reinforcement learning perspective with a parameterized policy that we theoretically prove to act as a dynamic linear surrogate maximizing the constructive interference of gradients. To enhance practical flexibility, AC-ODM supports two operational modes: (i) a proxy mode for fixed, pre-prepared corpora, where a policy learned on a small model is transferred to a larger target; and (ii) a non-proxy mode for direct end-to-end training from scratch without priors. Empirically, AC-ODM significantly outperforms prior methods in convergence speed and downstream accuracy across various architectures. On Pythia-1B, it reaches optimal validation perplexity using up to 66% fewer training steps than competitive baselines, delivering a 27.5% relative improvement in MMLU accuracy and a 2.23 x higher pass@1 on HumanEval, all while incurring a virtually negligible (0.4%) per-step wall-clock increase and only 2% additional memory overhead. Code is available at https://github.com/DANG-ai/AC-ODM.
affiliations: China Electronics Technology Group Corporation 15th Research Institute

ru.title: Интеллектуальное смешивание данных через обучение с подкреплением для эффективного обучения LLM
ru.desc: AC-ODM — это метод оптимизации состава предварительных данных для LLM с использованием обучения с подкреплением. Авторы предлагают динамическое смешивание данных через параметризованную политику Actor-Critic, которая действует как суррогат максимизирующий конструктивную интерференцию градиентов. Метод поддерживает два режима работы: проксирующий режим для переноса политики с маленькой модели на большую, и режим прямого обучения с нуля. Результаты показывают значительное улучшение скорости сходимости на 66% при сохранении вычислительной эффективности с минимальными накладными расходами по памяти и времени.

en.title: Reinforcement Learning for Optimized Pretraining Data in LLMs
en.desc: The paper presents AC-ODM, a method that optimizes the composition of pretraining data for large language models (LLMs) using reinforcement learning. This approach enhances the convergence speed and accuracy of LLMs while ensuring computational efficiency. AC-ODM introduces a dynamic data mixing strategy that adapts to training dynamics, outperforming traditional static methods. It offers two operational modes for flexibility, allowing for both fixed and end-to-end training scenarios, leading to significant improvements in model performance with minimal additional resource costs.

zh.title: 优化预训练数据组合，提升大语言模型性能
zh.desc: AC-ODM是一种通过强化学习优化大语言模型（LLM）预训练数据组合的方法，旨在提高收敛速度和下游任务的准确性，同时保持计算效率。该方法引入了演员-评论家在线数据混合策略，从强化学习的角度出发，理论上证明其能够最大化梯度的建设性干扰。AC-ODM支持两种操作模式：一种是代理模式，适用于固定的预先准备的数据集；另一种是非代理模式，允许从头开始进行端到端的训练。实验结果表明，AC-ODM在收敛速度和下游准确性方面显著优于现有方法，尤其在Pythia-1B上，训练步骤减少了66%，同时提高了MMLU准确性和HumanEval的通过率。

papers.title: ShotcreteDepth: A Bi-modal Dataset for Robust Robotic Depth Perception in Shotcrete Construction Environments
papers.abstract: Abstract A bi-modal construction domain dataset combining stereo RGB and LiDAR data under challenging environmental conditions is introduced for autonomous system perception research.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce ShotcreteDepth, a bi-modal dataset from the construction domain that captures both an active shotcreting process and general construction environments. The dataset comprises stereo RGB imagery and LiDAR point clouds acquired under harsh real-world conditions, including high turbidity and poor illumination. Such conditions adversely affect sensor measurements, leading to incomplete and noisy observations that pose significant challenges for perception systems in autonomous applications. Alongside the dataset, we release a lightweight annotation tool designed for time-efficient labeling of LiDAR point clouds. ShotcreteDepth consists of 11,252 temporally synchronized data samples, of which 220 are annotated for evaluation purposes. The dataset supports research in stereo matching, depth completion, and depth estimation under conditions that closely reflect the operational complexities found in industrial settings. Project repository: https://github.com/dtu-pas/shotcrete-depth
affiliations: Pioneer Centre for AI, Copenhagen, Denmark
Technical University of Denmark, Department of Electrical and Photonics Engineering

ru.title: Восприятие в стройке: мультимодальный датасет для автономных систем в экстремальных условиях
ru.desc: В работе представлен мультимодальный датасет ShotcreteDepth для исследования восприятия в сложных условиях строительной площадки, включающий синхронизированные стерео RGB-изображения и облака точек LiDAR. Датасет содержит 11 252 образца, собранные в экстремальных условиях с высокой мутностью и плохой освещённостью, которые вызывают неполные и зашумлённые измерения датчиков. Исследователи также разработали лёгкий инструмент аннотации для эффективной разметки облаков точек LiDAR. Датасет предназначен для развития методов стереоматчинга, заполнения карт глубины и оценки глубины в условиях, отражающих реальную сложность индустриальных приложений автономных систем.

en.title: Enhancing Autonomous Perception with ShotcreteDepth Dataset
en.desc: The paper presents ShotcreteDepth, a new dataset designed for improving perception in autonomous systems within the construction industry. It combines stereo RGB images and LiDAR point clouds, collected under difficult conditions like poor lighting and high turbidity, which challenge sensor accuracy. The dataset includes over 11,000 synchronized samples, with a subset annotated for evaluation, facilitating research in stereo matching, depth completion, and depth estimation. Additionally, a lightweight annotation tool is provided to streamline the labeling process for LiDAR data.

zh.title: 双模态数据集助力自主系统感知研究
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个名为ShotcreteDepth的双模态数据集，结合了立体RGB图像和LiDAR点云数据，旨在支持自主系统的感知研究。该数据集捕捉了活跃的喷射混凝土过程和一般建筑环境，数据采集在高浑浊度和低光照等恶劣条件下进行。由于这些条件对传感器测量产生负面影响，导致观测数据不完整且噪声较大，这对自主应用中的感知系统构成了重大挑战。数据集中包含11,252个时间同步的数据样本，其中220个样本经过标注以供评估，支持立体匹配、深度补全和深度估计等研究。

papers.title: An Exploratory Case Study of LLM-Assisted Refactoring and Gameplay Feature Generation in an Endless Runner Game
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models demonstrate varying effectiveness in software development tasks, successfully completing localized refactoring but showing limitations in integrating new gameplay features within existing game systems.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to support software development, but their practical usefulness in applied game-development settings remains underexplored, especially when generated code must be integrated into an existing game software system. This paper presents an exploratory empirical case study of GPT-4o in a custom Python/Pygame endless runner. The study examines six selected development tasks: three localized refactoring tasks and three tasks involving gameplay feature generation. The resulting implementations were evaluated using software metrics, unit tests, and manual gameplay assessments. In this case study, all three selected refactoring tasks were completed successfully in functional terms, whereas only one of the three selected gameplay feature generation tasks resulted in a correctly integrated feature. The findings suggest that, in this setting, GPT-4o handled localized transformations more reliably than tasks requiring new gameplay interactions across multiple existing systems. Given the exploratory single-case design, these results are best interpreted as indicative observations rather than as generalizable evidence of category-level model performance. Overall, the paper contributes a transparent case-based account of the opportunities and limitations of LLM-assisted refactoring and gameplay feature generation in an existing game software system.
affiliations: IU International University of Applied Sciences, Erfurt, Germany

ru.title: LLM хорош для рефакторинга, но не для интеграции новых игровых механик
ru.desc: В статье исследуется эффективность большой языковой модели GPT-4o для задач разработки программного обеспечения на примере простой игры на Python/Pygame. Авторы оценили способность модели выполнять три задачи рефакторинга кода и три задачи создания новых игровых механик, используя метрики качества, модульные тесты и ручную оценку геймплея. Результаты показали, что GPT-4o успешно справляется с локализованными трансформациями кода, но испытывает значительные затруднения при интеграции новых функций в существующую архитектуру игровой системы. Исследование дает практические insights о возможностях и ограничениях применения LLM-моделей при разработке игр.

en.title: Unlocking Code: LLMs Shine in Refactoring, Struggle with Gameplay Features
en.desc: This paper explores the effectiveness of large language models (LLMs), specifically GPT-4o, in software development tasks within game development. It focuses on two types of tasks: localized refactoring and gameplay feature generation, evaluating their success through software metrics and gameplay assessments. The study found that while the model excelled at refactoring tasks, it struggled with integrating new gameplay features into existing systems. The results highlight the potential and limitations of using LLMs in practical game development scenarios, suggesting that they are more reliable for localized code changes than for complex feature integrations.

zh.title: 大型语言模型在游戏开发中的机遇与挑战
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在软件开发中的应用，特别是在游戏开发中的有效性。研究通过一个案例研究，分析了GPT-4o在Python/Pygame无尽跑者游戏中的表现，重点关注局部重构和游戏功能生成任务。结果显示，GPT-4o在局部重构任务中表现良好，但在生成新游戏功能时存在整合困难。论文的发现表明，LLMs在处理现有系统中的局部变换时更为可靠，而在需要跨多个系统的新游戏交互时则表现有限。

papers.title: Improving Text-to-Music Generation with Human Preference Rewards
papers.abstract: Abstract A text-to-music generation system uses reward conditioning, expert iteration, and preference tuning to improve audio quality while maintaining efficiency within a 120M-parameter model framework.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We describe our entry to the efficiency track of the Academic Text-to-Music (ATTM) Grand Challenge at ICME 2026. Beyond the challenge protocol's FAD-CLAP and CLAP score, we add a learned human-preference reward from TuneJury, a twin pairwise ranker trained over open music-preference datasets. The reward serves both as a training-time conditioning signal and as a sample-selection criterion. The pipeline combines five engineering decisions on a 120M-parameter FluxAudio-S backbone, four at training time and one at inference: (i) training-time reward conditioning that doubles as an inference-time CFG axis, (ii) a sweep over five score-conditioning architectures, where training and inference use different variants, (iii) expert iteration on the top decile, (iv) a short preference-tuning pass (CRPO) for audio-text alignment, and (v) inference post-processing via joint CFG, source separation, and loudness normalization. Per-stage decomposition on 100 Song Describer prompts shows training-time reward conditioning as a functional conditioning axis, expert iteration as the dominant contributor, the preference-tuning pass adding only noise-level gain, and the inference-time score scalar already saturated by the end of the chain.

ru.title: Эффективная генерация музыки через кондиционирование вознаграждением и обучение человеческим предпочтениям
ru.desc: В работе предлагается система генерации музыки из текста, использующая кондиционирование вознаграждением для улучшения качества звука. Авторы применяют метод итерации экспертов и настройку предпочтений на базе модели FluxAudio-S с 120 миллионами параметров. Система интегрирует механизм обучения человеческим предпочтениям через TuneJury — двойной попарный ранжировщик, обученный на открытых датасетах музыкальных предпочтений. Комплексный пайплайн включает пять инженерных решений: кондиционирование вознаграждением, поиск оптимальной архитектуры кондиционирования, отбор лучших образцов, настройку предпочтений через CRPO и постобработку при инференсе.

en.title: Efficient Text-to-Music Generation with Reward Conditioning
en.desc: This paper presents a text-to-music generation system that enhances audio quality while being efficient, using a model with 120 million parameters. It incorporates reward conditioning, expert iteration, and preference tuning to optimize the music generation process. The system utilizes a learned human-preference reward to guide both training and sample selection, improving alignment between audio and text. The results indicate that expert iteration significantly contributes to performance, while preference tuning has a minimal impact on audio quality.

zh.title: 高效文本到音乐生成的创新之路
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种文本到音乐生成系统，利用奖励条件、专家迭代和偏好调优来提高音频质量，同时保持在120M参数模型框架内的效率。该系统在ICME 2026的学术文本到音乐（ATTM）大挑战中参赛，除了使用挑战协议的FAD-CLAP和CLAP评分外，还引入了来自TuneJury的学习人类偏好奖励。这个奖励不仅作为训练时的条件信号，还作为样本选择的标准。通过对100个歌曲描述提示的逐阶段分解，结果显示训练时的奖励条件作为功能性条件轴，专家迭代是主要贡献者，而偏好调优的增益仅为噪声水平的提升。

papers.title: Toward Parking Spot Occupancy Recognition: A Self-Supervised Approach
papers.abstract: Abstract A self-supervised transfer learning approach for parking spot occupancy recognition that achieves high accuracy with minimal labeled data through two-stage training and deployment strategies.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As urban areas expand, automatic monitoring of parking lots becomes essential for efficient and sustainable cities. This work proposes a self-supervised approach for parking spot occupancy recognition that requires no labeled samples from the target parking lot. Building upon a self-supervised transfer learning fine-tuning protocol, the proposed training strategy consists of two self-supervised stages: first on unlabeled generic data and then on unlabeled target-specific data, followed by supervised fine-tuning using only generic parking lot labels. We adopt SimCLR with a ResNet-50 encoder and evaluate the method under a leave-one-out cross-environment protocol on three public datasets: PKLot, CNRPark-EXT, and PLds. We also introduce a two-stage deployment strategy in which a Strong General Model is initially deployed, followed by a Specialized Model that incorporates unlabeled images collected during the first N days of deployment in a self-supervised manner. Experimental results show that the Strong General Model alone outperforms supervised and self-supervised baselines, achieving an average accuracy of 97.2%, which further improves to 97.8% with the proposed two-stage strategy. These results demonstrate that self-supervised learning enables a scalable and labelefficient solution for real-world parking occupancy monitoring. Our trained models and source code are publicly available at https://github.com/LoanMaikon/Parking-Spot-Occupancy-Recognition.

ru.title: Самообучаемое трансферное обучение для мониторинга парковок без разметки
ru.desc: Работа предлагает самообучаемый подход на основе трансферного обучения для распознавания занятости парковочных мест без использования размеченных данных с целевой парковки. Метод использует двухэтапное обучение: сначала на немаркированных общих данных с помощью SimCLR и ResNet-50, затем на немаркированных данных целевой парковки, и завершается супервизируемой фильтровкой на общих размеченных примерах. Предложена также двухэтапная стратегия развертывания, где сначала используется общая модель, а затем специализированная модель дополняется немаркированными изображениями, собранными во время развертывания. Экспериментальные результаты на трех публичных датасетах показывают точность 97.2% для общей модели и 97.8% с предложенной двухэтапной стратегией.

en.title: Efficient Parking Spot Recognition with Self-Supervised Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a self-supervised transfer learning method for recognizing parking spot occupancy, which minimizes the need for labeled data. The approach involves a two-stage training process: first, it trains on generic unlabeled data, then on target-specific unlabeled data, before fine-tuning with minimal labeled examples. The model utilizes SimCLR with a ResNet-50 encoder and is evaluated using a leave-one-out cross-environment strategy across three datasets. Results indicate that the proposed method achieves high accuracy, demonstrating the effectiveness of self-supervised learning in practical applications for parking monitoring.

zh.title: 自监督学习助力停车位占用识别的高效解决方案
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种自监督迁移学习的方法，用于停车位占用识别，能够在标注数据极少的情况下实现高准确率。该方法采用两阶段的训练和部署策略，首先在未标注的通用数据上进行自监督训练，然后在目标特定的未标注数据上进行微调，最后使用通用停车场标签进行监督微调。我们使用SimCLR和ResNet-50编码器，并在三个公共数据集上进行评估，结果显示该方法的准确率达到了97.2%。通过引入两阶段的部署策略，最终准确率进一步提高到97.8%，证明了自监督学习在实际停车占用监测中的可扩展性和高效性。
